=============================================================================\n1297\t\n1298\t// StatusCodeValidationResult provides detailed information about status code validation.\n1299\t// It includes not just whether validation passed, but also the expected vs actual values\n1300\t// and detailed mismatch information to help diagnose validation failures.\n1301\ttype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\n1302\t\t// Valid indicates whether the validation passed\n1303\t\tValid bool\n1304\t\t// ActualCode is the HTTP status code from the response\n1305\t\tActualCode int\n1306\t\t// ExpectedCodes contains the expected status code(s) that were validated against\n1307\t\tExpectedCodes []int\n1308\t\t// MatchedCode is the specific expected code that matched (if any)\n1309\t\tMatchedCode *int\n1310\t\t// MismatchDetails contains human-readable information about why validation failed\n1311\t\tMismatchDetails string\n1312\t\t// IsClientError indicates whether the actual code is a client error (4xx)\n1313\t\tIsClientError bool\n1314\t\t// IsServerError indicates whether the actual code is a server error (5xx)\n1315\t\tIsServerError bool\n1316\t\t// Category describes the general category of the actual status code\n1317\t\tCategory string\n1318\t}\n1319\t\n1320\t// ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails performs comprehensive status code validation with detailed results.\n1321\t// Unlike HTTPStatusCodeIsValid which returns only a boolean, this function provides detailed information\n1322\t// about the validation process, making it easier to diagnose why validation failed.\n1323\t//\n1324\t// Parameters:\n1325\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1326\t//   - expected: Expected status code(s) - can be a single int or []int\n1327\t//   - requestContext: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"POST /api/users\", \"GET /api/resource/123\")\n1328\t//\n1329\t// Returns a StatusCodeValidationResult with detailed validation information.\n1330\t//\n1331\t// Example usage:\n1332\t//\n1333\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, []int{200, 201, 204}, \"POST /api/users\")\n1334\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1335\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %s\", result.MismatchDetails)\n1336\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Got: %d, expected one of: %v\", result.ActualCode, result.ExpectedCodes)\n1337\t//\t}\n1338\t//\n1339\t//\t// Single code validation without context\n1340\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, 200, \"\")\n1341\t//\tif result.Valid {\n1342\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status code is valid: %d\", result.ActualCode)\n1343\t//\t}\n1344\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}, requestContext ...string) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1345\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1346\t\t\tValid:             false,\n1347\t\t\tActualCode:        0,\n1348\t\t\tExpectedCodes:     []int{},\n1349\t\t\tMatchedCode:       nil,\n1350\t\t\tMismatchDetails:   \"\",\n1351\t\t\tIsClientError:     false,\n1352\t\t\tIsServerError:     false,\n1353\t\t\tCategory:          \"unknown\",\n1354\t\t}\n1355\t\n1356\t\tif resp == nil {\n1357\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"response is nil\"\n1358\t\t\treturn result\n1359\t\t}\n1360\t\n1361\t\tresult.ActualCode = resp.StatusCode\n1362\t\n1363\t\t// Set error flags and category\n1364\t\tswitch {\n1365\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500:\n1366\t\t\tresult.IsClientError = true\n1367\t\t\tresult.Category = \"client_error\"\n1368\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600:\n1369\t\t\tresult.IsServerError = true\n1370\t\t\tresult.Category = \"server_error\"\n1371\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 200 && resp.StatusCode < 300:\n1372\t\t\tresult.Category = \"success\"\n1373\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 300 && resp.StatusCode < 400:\n1374\t\t\tresult.Category = \"redirection\"\n1375\t\tdefault:\n1376\t\t\tresult.Category = \"other\"\n1377\t\t}\n1378\t\n1379\t\t// Parse expected codes\n1380\t\tvar expectedCodes []int\n1381\t\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n1382\t\tcase int:\n1383\t\t\texpectedCodes = []int{v}\n1384\t\tcase []int:\n1385\t\t\texpectedCodes = v\n1386\t\tdefault:\n1387\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid expected type: %T\", expected)\n1388\t\t\treturn result\n1389\t\t}\n1390\t\n1391\t\tresult.ExpectedCodes = expectedCodes\n1392\t\n1393\t\t// Check for match\n1394\t\tfor _, code := range expectedCodes {\n1395\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode == code {\n1396\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = true\n1397\t\t\t\tresult.MatchedCode = &code\n1398\t\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"\"\n1399\t\t\t\treturn result\n1400\t\t\t}\n1401\t\t}\n1402\t\n1403\t\t// Extract optional context\n1404\t\tcontext := \"\"\n1405\t\tif len(requestContext) > 0 && requestContext[0] != \"\" {\n1406\t\t\tcontext = requestContext[0]\n1407\t\t}\n1408\t\n1409\t\t// Build comprehensive mismatch information with suggestions\n1410\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails(\n1411\t\t\texpectedCodes, resp.StatusCode, result.Category, result.IsClientError, result.IsServerError, context)\n1412\t\n1413\t\treturn result\n1414\t}\n1415\t\n1416\t// buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails creates detailed mismatch information including\n1417\t// status code category, context, and specific suggestions based on the actual code.\n1418\t// It uses FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent formatting across the codebase.\n1419\tfunc buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails(expectedCodes []int, actualCode int, category string, isClientError, isServerError bool, context string) string {\n1420\t\t// Build validation details that include status code category information\n1421\t\tvar validationDetails []string\n1422\t\n1423\t\t// Add status code category information to validation details\n1424\t\tcategoryDesc := getCategoryDescription(category)\n1425\t\tif categoryDesc != \"\" {\n1426\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code category: %s\", categoryDesc))\n1427\t\t}\n1428\t\n1429\t\t// Prepare range info with category details\n1430\t\tvar rangeInfo string\n1431\t\tif isClientError {\n1432\t\t\trangeInfo = \"400-499 (Client Error)\"\n1433\t\t} else if isServerError {\n1434\t\t\trangeInfo = \"500-599 (Server Error)\"\n1435\t\t} else if category == \"success\" {\n1436\t\t\trangeInfo = \"200-299 (Success)\"\n1437\t\t} else if category == \"redirection\" {\n1438\t\t\trangeInfo = \"300-399 (Redirection)\"\n1439\t\t}\n1440\t\n1441\t\t// Determine expected value for FormatValidationErrorWithDetails\n1442\t\tvar expected interface{}\n1443\t\tif len(expectedCodes) == 1 {\n1444\t\t\texpected = expectedCodes[0]\n1445\t\t} else {\n1446\t\t\texpected = expectedCodes\n1447\t\t}\n1448\t\n1449\t\t// Use FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent error formatting\n1450\t\tvalidationErr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n1451\t\t\t\"status_code\",\n1452\t\t\texpected,\n1453\t\t\tactualCode,\n1454\t\t\tcontext,\n1455\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n1456\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n1457\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n1458\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n1459\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails - not applicable for status code validation\n1460\t\t\trangeInfo,\n1461\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n1462\t\t)\n1463\t\n1464\t\t// Return the formatted error string\n1465\t\treturn validationErr.Error()\n1466\t}\n1467\t\n1468\t// getCategoryDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code category.\n1469\tfunc getCategoryDescription(category string) string {\n1470\t\tdescriptions := map[string]string{\n1471\t\t\t\"success\":       \"2xx - Successful responses\",\n1472\t\t\t\"redirection\":   \"3xx - Redirection messages\",\n1473\t\t\t\"client_error\":  \"4xx - Client error responses\",\n1474\t\t\t\"server_error\":  \"5xx - Server error responses\",\n1475\t\t\t\"other\":         \"Non-standard status codes\",\n1476\t\t}\n1477\t\treturn descriptions[category]\n1478\t}\n1479\t\n1480\t// getStatusCodeSpecificSuggestions returns relevant suggestions based on the specific status code received.\n1481\tfunc getStatusCodeSpecificSuggestions(code int) []string {\n1482\t\tswitch code {\n1483\t\tcase 400:\n1484\t\t\treturn []string{\n1485\t\t\t\t\"Check request body syntax and formatting\",\n1486\t\t\t\t\"Verify all required fields are present\",\n1487\t\t\t\t\"Ensure Content-Type header matches request body format\",\n1488\t\t\t\t\"Validate JSON schema if API documentation for required permissions\",\n1502\t\t\t\t\"Ensure proper OAuth scopes are granted\",\n1503\t\t\t}\n1504\t\tcase 404:\n1505\t\t\treturn []string{\n1506\t\t\t\t\"Verify the endpoint []string{\n1541\t\t\t\t\"Implement retry with exponential backoff\",\n1542\t\t\t\t\"Check service maintenance schedules\",\n1543\t\t\t\t\"Verify service capacity and scaling\",\n1544\t\t\t\t\"Review codes\n1555\t\t\tif code >= 400 && code < 500 {\n1556\t\t\t\treturn []string{\n1557\t\t\t\t\t\"Review request parameters and body\",\n1558\t\t\t\t\t\"Check API documentation for requirements\",\n1559\t\t\t\t\t\"Verify all headers are correctly set\",\n1560\t\t\t\t\t\"Ensure request complies with API contracts\",\n1561\t\t\t\t}\n1562\t\t\t}\n1563\t\t\tif code >= 500 && code < 600 {\n1564\t\t\t\treturn []string{\n1565\t\t\t\t\t\"Implement retry logic with exponential backoff\",\n1566\t\t\t\t\t\"Check service status page for ongoing issues\",\n1567\t\t\t\t\t\"Contact support range of status codes for flexible validation.\n1576\t// This is useful when you want to accept any status code within a certain range.\n1577\ttype StatusCodeRange struct {\n1578\t\t// Min is the minimum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1579\t\tMin int\n1580\t\t// Max is the maximum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1581\t\tMax int\n1582\t\t// Description is a human-readable description of what this range represents\n1583\t\tDescription string\n1584\t}\n1585\t\n1586\t// Common status code ranges\n1587\tvar (\n1588\t\t// Range1xx covers informational responses (100-199)\n1589\t\tRange1xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 100, Max: 199, Description: \"Informational\"}\n1590\t\t// Range2xx covers successful responses (200-299)\n1591\t\tRange2xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 299, Description: \"Success\"}\n1592\t\t// Range3xx covers redirection responses (300-399)\n1593\t\tRange3xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 300, Max: 399, Description: \"Redirection\"}\n1594\t\t// Range4xx covers client error responses (400-499)\n1595\t\tRange4xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 400, Max: 499, Description: \"Client Error\"}\n1596\t\t// Range5xx covers server error responses (500-599)\n1597\t\tRange5xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 500, Max: 599, Description: \"Server Error\"}\n1598\t)\n1599\t\n1600\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange checks if a response's status code falls within a specified range.\n1601\t// This is useful for flexible validation where you want to accept any status code within a category.\n1602\t//\n1603\t// Parameters:\n1604\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1605\t//   - range: The status code range to validate against\n1606\t//\n1607\t// Returns true if the response status code falls within the range, false otherwise.\n1608\t// Returns false if the response is nil or the range is invalid (Min > Max).\n1609\t//\n1610\t// Example usage:\n1611\t//\n1612\t//\t// Accept any success code (2xx)\n1613\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range2xx) {\n1614\t//\t    // Response is successful (200-299)\n1615\t//\t}\n1616\t//\n1617\t//\t// Accept any client error (4xx)\n1618\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range4xx) {\n1619\t//\t    // Response is a client error (400-499)\n1620\t//\t}\n1621\t//\n1622\t//\t// Custom range for specific validation\n1623\t//\tcustomRange := StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 204, Description: \"Standard success\"}\n1624\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, customRange) {\n1625\t//\t    // Response is in custom range\n1626\t//\t}\n1627\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(resp *http.Response, statusRange StatusCodeRange) bool {\n1628\t\tif resp == nil {\n1629\t\t\treturn false\n1630\t\t}\n1631\t\n1632\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1633\t\t\treturn false\n1634\t\t}\n1635\t\n1636\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= statusRange.Min && resp.StatusCode <= statusRange.Max\n1637\t}\n1638\t\n1639\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails provides detailed status code range validation.\n1640\t// Like ValidateStatusCodeRange but with comprehensive result information including\n1641\t// range boundaries, distance from valid range, and contextual suggestions.\n1642\t//\n1643\t// Parameters:\n1644\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1645\t//   - range: The status code range to validate against\n1646\t//\n1647\t// Returns a context (e.g., '4xx', '5xx'), and suggestions for fixing the issue.\n1650\t//\n1651\t// Example usage:\n1652\t//\n1653\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails(response, Range4xx)\n1654\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1655\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status code %d is not in range %s (%d-%d)\",\n1656\t//\t        result.ActualCode, result.Category, range.Min, range.Max)\n1657\t//\t    // MismatchDetails now includes:\n1658\t//\t    // - Expected range with description (e.g., \"400-499 Client Error\")\n1659\t//\t    // - Actual received status code\n1660\t//\t    // - Distance from valid range (e.g., \"99 codes below range\")\n1661\t//\t    // - Range pattern context (e.g., \"Validating 4xx pattern\")\n1662\t//\t    // - Specific suggestions based on the actual code\n1663\t//\t}\n1664\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, statusRange StatusCodeRange) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1665\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1666\t\t\tValid:           false,\n1667\t\t\tActualCode:      0,\n1668\t\t\tExpectedCodes:   []int{statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max},\n1669\t\t\tMatchedCode:     nil,\n1670\t\t\tMismatchDetails: \"\",\n1671\t\t\tIsClientError:   false,\n1672\t\t\tIsServerError:   false,\n1673\t\t\tCategory:        statusRange.Description,\n1674\t\t}\n1675\t\n1676\t\tif resp == nil {\n1677\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"response is nil\"\n1678\t\t\treturn result\n1679\t\t}\n1680\t\n1681\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1682\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid range: Min (%d) > Max (%d)\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max)\n1683\t\t\treturn result\n1684\t\t}\n1685\t\n1686\t\tresult.ActualCode = resp.StatusCode\n1687\t\n1688\t\t// Check range\n1689\t\tinRange := resp.StatusCode >= statusRange.Min && resp.StatusCode <= statusRange.Max\n1690\t\tresult.Valid = inRange\n1691\t\n1692\t\t// Set error flags\n1693\t\tif resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500 {\n1694\t\t\tresult.IsClientError = true\n1695\t\t}\n1696\t\tif resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600 {\n1697\t\t\tresult.IsServerError = true\n1698\t\t}\n1699\t\n1700\t\tif !inRange {\n1701\t\t\t// Build comprehensive mismatch details with range boundaries, distance, and suggestions\n1702\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = buildRangeMismatchDetails(resp.StatusCode, statusRange)\n1703\t\t}\n1704\t\n1705\t\treturn result\n1706\t}\n1707\t\n1708\t// buildRangeMismatchDetails creates comprehensive mismatch information for range validation failures.\n1709\t// This function calculates the distance from the valid range, identifies the range pattern context,\n1710\t// and generates detailed error messages with suggestions using FormatValidationErrorWithDetails.\n1711\tfunc buildRangeMismatchDetails(actualCode int, statusRange StatusCodeRange) string {\n1712\t\t// Calculate distance from valid range\n1713\t\tvar distanceInfo string\n1714\t\tvar distanceDetails []string\n1715\t\n1716\t\tif actualCode < statusRange.Min {\n1717\t\t\tdistance := statusRange.Min - actualCode\n1718\t\t\tif distance == 1 {\n1719\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d code below range\", distance)\n1720\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d code below the minimum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Min))\n1721\t\t\t} else {\n1722\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes below range\", distance)\n1723\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes below the minimum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Min))\n1724\t\t\t}\n1725\t\t} else {\n1726\t\t\tdistance := actualCode - statusRange.Max\n1727\t\t\tif distance == 1 {\n1728\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d code above range\", distance)\n1729\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d code above the maximum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Max))\n1730\t\t\t} else {\n1731\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes above range\", distance)\n1732\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes above the maximum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Max))\n1733\t\t\t}\n1734\t\t}\n1735\t\n1736\t\t// Determine range pattern context (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n1737\t\trangePattern := getRangePattern(statusRange)\n1738\t\n1739\t\t// Build expected range with description\n1740\t\texpectedRange := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max)\n1741\t\tif statusRange.Description != \"\" {\n1742\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", expectedRange, statusRange.Description)\n1743\t\t}\n1744\t\n1745\t\t// Add pattern context to expected range\n1746\t\tif rangePattern != \"\" {\n1747\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s - %s pattern\", expectedRange, rangePattern)\n1748\t\t}\n1749\t\n1750\t\t// Build validation details\n1751\t\tvalidationDetails := []string{\n1752\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Expected range: %d-%d %s\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max, statusRange.Description),\n1753\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Actual status code: %d\", actualCode),\n1754\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Distance from valid range: %s\", distanceInfo),\n1755\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern context: %s\", rangePattern),\n1756\t\t}\n1757\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, distanceDetails...)\n1758\t\n1759\t\t// Use FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent formatting\n1760\t\tvalidationErr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n1761\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1762\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1763\t\t\tactualCode,\n1764\t\t\t\"\", // context - not applicable for range validation\n1765\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n1766\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n1767\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n1768\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n1769\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern: %s, Distance: %s\", rangePattern, distanceInfo),\n1770\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1771\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n1772\t\t)\n1773\t\n1774\t\treturn validationErr.Error()\n1775\t}\n1776\t\n1777\t// getRangePattern identifies the range pattern from a StatusCodeRange.\n1778\t// Returns patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '2xx', etc. based on the range boundaries.\n1779\tfunc getRangePattern(statusRange StatusCodeRange) string {\n1780\t\tmin := statusRange.Min\n1781\t\tmax := statusRange.Max\n1782\t\n1783\t\t// Check for standard century ranges\n1784\t\tif min/100 == max/100 && min%100 == 0 && max%100 == 99 {\n1785\t\t\tcentury := min / 100\n1786\t\t\tswitch century {\n1787\t\t\tcase 1:\n1788\t\t\t\treturn \"1xx (Informational)\"\n1789\t\t\tcase 2:\n1790\t\t\t\treturn \"2xx (Success)\"\n1791\t\t\tcase 3:\n1792\t\t\t\treturn \"3xx (Redirection)\"\n1793\t\t\tcase 4:\n1794\t\t\t\treturn \"4xx (Client Error)\"\n1795\t\t\tcase 5:\n1796\t\t\t\treturn \"5xx (Server Error)\"\n1797\t\t\t}\n1798\t\t}\n1799\t\n1800\t\t// For custom ranges, provide the pattern context\n1801\t\tif min/100 == max/100 {\n1802\t\t\tcentury := min / 100\n1803\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%dxx range (custom)\", century)\n1804\t\t}\n1805\t\n1806\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d range (custom)\", min/100, max/100)\n1807\t}\n1808\t\n1809\t// =============================================================================\n1810\t// ENHANCED ERROR MESSAGE VALIDATION\n1811\t// =============================================================================\n1812\t\n1813\t// EnhancedErrorMessagePattern defines a pattern for matching error messages in response bodies.\n1814\ttype EnhancedErrorMessagePattern struct {\n1815\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check in the response body\n1816\t\tFieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames\n1817\t\t// Pattern is a regular expression pattern that the error message must match\n1818\t\tPattern string\n1819\t\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether pattern matching should be case-insensitive\n1820\t\tCaseInsensitive bool\n1821\t\t// MustContain specifies strings that must be present in the error message\n1822\t\tMustContain []string\n1823\t\t// MustNotContain specifies strings that must NOT be present in the error message\n1824\t\tMustNotContain []string\n1825\t\t// MinLength is the minimum length of the error message (0 for no minimum)\n1826\t\tMinLength int\n1827\t\t// MaxLength is the maximum length of the error message (0 for no maximum)\n1828\t\tMaxLength int\n1829\t}\n1830\t\n1831\t// ErrorMessageValidationResult provides detailed information about error message validation.\n1832\ttype ErrorMessageValidationResult struct {\n1833\t\t// Valid indicates whether the error message validation passed\n1834\t\tValid bool\n1835\t\t// Found indicates whether an error message field was found in the response\n1836\t\tFound bool\n1837\t\t// Message is the actual error message content that was found\n1838\t\tMessage string\n1839\t\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error message was found\n1840\t\tFieldName string\n1841\t\t// PatternMatched indicates whether the regex pattern matched\n1842\t\tPatternMatched bool\n1843\t\t// MustContainResults shows which required strings were found\n1844\t\tMustContainResults map[string]bool\n1845\t\t// MustNotContainResults shows which forbidden strings were found (should all be false)\n1846\t\tMustNotContainResults map[string]bool\n1847\t\t// LengthValidation indicates whether the message length was valid\n1848\t\tLengthValidation bool\n1849\t\t// Issues contains a list of validation issues found\n1850\t\tIssues []string\n1851\t}\n1852\t\n1853\t// ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails performs comprehensive error message content validation.\n1854\t// It checks for the presence of error messages and validates their content against various criteria.\n1855\t//\n1856\t// Parameters:\n1857\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body (should be a map[string]interface{})\n1858\t//   - pattern: The validation pattern to apply\n1859\t//\n1860\t// Returns an ErrorMessageValidationResult with detailed validation information.\n1861\t//\n1862\t// Example usage:\n1863\t//\n1864\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n1865\t//\tpattern := EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n1866\t//\t    FieldNames: DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames(),\n1867\t//\t    MustContain: []string{\"invalid\", \"token\"},\n1868\t//\t    MustNotContain: []string{\"password\", \"secret\"},\n1869\t//\t    MinLength: 10,\n1870\t//\t}\n1871\t//\tresult := ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(body, pattern)\n1872\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1873\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Error message validation failed: %v\", result.Issues)\n1874\t//\t}\n1875\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(responseBody interface{}, pattern EnhancedErrorMessagePattern) ErrorMessageValidationResult {\n1876\t\tresult := ErrorMessageValidationResult{\n1877\t\t\tValid:                 false,\n1878\t\t\tFound:                 false,\n1879\t\t\tMessage:               \"\",\n1880\t\t\tFieldName:             \"\",\n1881\t\t\tPatternMatched:        false,\n1882\t\t\tMustContainResults:    make(map[string]bool),\n1883\t\t\tMustNotContainResults: make(map[string]bool),\n1884\t\t\tLengthValidation:      true,\n1885\t\t\tIssues:                []string{},\n1886\t\t}\n1887\t\n1888\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1889\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is nil\")\n1890\t\t\treturn result\n1891\t\t}\n1892\t\n1893\t\t// Use default field names if not provided\n1894\t\tfieldNames := pattern.FieldNames\n1895\t\tif fieldNames == nil {\n1896\t\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n1897\t\t}\n1898\t\n1899\t\t// Try to convert to map\n1900\t\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\n1901\t\tif !ok {\n1902\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is not a map\")\n1903\t\t\treturn result\n1904\t\t}\n1905\t\n1906\t\t// Search for error message in specified fields\n1907\t\tvar foundMessage string\n1908\t\tvar foundFieldName string\n1909\t\n1910\t\t// Check primary field\n1911\t\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \"\" {\n1912\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\n1913\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1914\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessage = strValue\n1915\t\t\t\t\tfoundFieldName = fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName\n1916\t\t\t\t}\n1917\t\t\t}\n1918\t\t}\n1919\t\n1920\t\t// Check secondary field if not found in primary\n1921\t\tif foundMessage == \"\" && fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \"\" {\n1922\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\n1923\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1924\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessage = strValue\n1925\t\t\t\t\tfoundFieldName = fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName\n1926\t\t\t\t}\n1927\t\t\t}\n1928\t\t}\n1929\t\n1930\t\tresult.Found = foundMessage != \"\"\n1931\t\tresult.Message = foundMessage\n1932\t\tresult.FieldName = foundFieldName\n1933\t\n1934\t\tif !result.Found {\n1935\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"no valid error message field found\")\n1936\t\t\treturn result\n1937\t\t}\n1938\t\n1939\t\t// Validate length\n1940\t\tif pattern.MinLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) < pattern.MinLength {\n1941\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1942\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d is less than minimum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MinLength))\n1943\t\t}\n1944\t\n1945\t\tif pattern.MaxLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) > pattern.MaxLength {\n1946\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1947\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d exceeds maximum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MaxLength))\n1948\t\t}\n1949\t\n1950\t\t// Validate regex pattern\n1951\t\tif pattern.Pattern != \"\" {\n1952\t\t\tregex := regexp.MustCompile(pattern.Pattern)\n1953\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1954\t\t\t\tregex = regexp.MustCompile(\"(?i)\" + pattern.Pattern)\n1955\t\t\t}\n1956\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = regex.MatchString(foundMessage)\n1957\t\t\tif !result.PatternMatched {\n1958\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not match required pattern: %s\", pattern.Pattern))\n1959\t\t\t}\n1960\t\t} else {\n1961\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = true // No pattern specified, consider it matched\n1962\t\t}\n1963\t\n1964\t\t// Validate MustContain strings\n1965\t\tallMustContainFound := true\n1966\t\tfor _, mustContain := range pattern.MustContain {\n1967\t\t\tvar found bool\n1968\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1969\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1970\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustContain))\n1971\t\t\t} else {\n1972\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustContain)\n1973\t\t\t}\n1974\t\t\tresult.MustContainResults[mustContain] = found\n1975\t\t\tif !found {\n1976\t\t\t\tallMustContainFound = false\n1977\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not contain required string: %s\", mustContain))\n1978\t\t\t}\n1979\t\t}\n1980\t\n1981\t\t// Validate MustNotContain strings\n1982\t\thasForbiddenStrings := false\n1983\t\tfor _, mustNotContain := range pattern.MustNotContain {\n1984\t\t\tvar found bool\n1985\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1986\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1987\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustNotContain))\n1988\t\t\t} else {\n1989\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustNotContain)\n1990\t\t\t}\n1991\t\t\tresult.MustNotContainResults[mustNotContain] = found\n1992\t\t\tif found {\n1993\t\t\t\thasForbiddenStrings = true\n1994\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message contains forbidden string: %s\", mustNotContain))\n1995\t\t\t}\n1996\t\t}\n1997\t\n1998\t\t// Overall validity\n1999\t\tresult.Valid = result.Found && result.LengthValidation && result.PatternMatched &&\n2000\t\t\tallMustContainFound && !hasForbiddenStrings\n2001\t\n2002\t\treturn result\n2003\t}\n2004\t\n2005\t// =============================================================================\n2006\t// ERROR CODE DETECTION\n2007\t// =============================================================================\n2008\t\n2009\t// ErrorCodeMatch represents a found error code in a response.\n2010\ttype ErrorCodeMatch struct {\n2011\t\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error code was found\n2012\t\tFieldName string\n2013\t\t// CodeValue is the actual error code value (as a string for flexibility)\n2014\t\tCodeValue string\n2015\t\t// NumericCode is the error code parsed as an integer (if applicable)\n2016\t\tNumericCode *int\n2017\t\t// MatchedPattern is the pattern that matched this code\n2018\t\tMatchedPattern string\n2019\t\t// Position describes where in the response the code was found\n2020\t\tPosition string\n2021\t}\n2022\t\n2023\t// EnhancedErrorCodePattern defines a pattern for finding error codes in responses.\n2024\ttype EnhancedErrorCodePattern struct {\n2025\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check for error codes\n2026\t\tFieldNames []string\n2027\t\t// NumericOnly indicates whether to only match numeric error codes\n2028\t\tNumericOnly bool\n2029\t\t// ValuePatterns contains regex patterns for valid error code values\n2030\t\tValuePatterns []string\n2031\t}\n2032\t\n2033\t// FindErrorCodesInResponse searches for error codes in an HTTP response body.\n2034\t// This is useful for validating that specific error codes are present in error responses.\n2035\t//\n2036\t// Parameters:\n2037\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body (should be a map[string]interface{})\n2038\t//   - patterns: The patterns to use for finding error codes\n2039\t//\n2040\t// Returns a slice of ErrorCodeMatch representing all found error codes.\n2041\t//\n2042\t// Example usage:\n2043\t//\n2044\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n2045\t//\tpatterns := []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n2046\t//\t    {\n2047\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error_code\", \"code\"},\n2048\t//\t        NumericOnly: true,\n2049\t//\t    },\n2050\t//\t    {\n2051\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error\"},\n2052\t//\t        ValuePatterns: []string{\"invalid_token\", \"access_denied\"},\n2053\t//\t    },\n2054\t//\t}\n2055\t//\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(body, patterns)\n2056\t//\tfor _, match := range matches {\n2057\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Found error code: %s in field: %s\", match.CodeValue, match.FieldName)\n2058\t//\t}\n2059\tfunc FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody interface{}, patterns []EnhancedErrorCodePattern) []ErrorCodeMatch {\n2060\t\tvar matches []ErrorCodeMatch\n2061\t\n2062\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n2063\t\t\treturn matches\n2064\t\t}\n2065\t\n2066\t\t// Try to convert to map\n2067\t\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\n2068\t\tif !ok {\n2069\t\t\treturn matches\n2070\t\t}\n2071\t\n2072\t\tfor _, pattern := range patterns {\n2073\t\t\t// Default to common error code fields if none specified\n2074\t\t\tfieldsToCheck := pattern.FieldNames\n2075\t\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n2076\t\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error_code\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n2077\t\t\t}\n2078\t\n2079\t\t\tfor _, fieldName := range fieldsToCheck {\n2080\t\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldName]; exists {\n2081\t\t\t\t\tvar codeValue string\n2082\t\t\t\t\tvar numericCode *int\n2083\t\t\t\t\tvar matchedPattern string\n2084\t\n2085\t\t\t\t\t// Handle different value types\n2086\t\t\t\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n2087\t\t\t\t\tcase string:\n2088\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = v\n2089\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"string\"\n2090\t\t\t\t\t\t// Try to parse as number\n2091\t\t\t\t\t\tif num, err := parseIntFromString(v); err == nil && pattern.NumericOnly {\n2092\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &num\n2093\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2094\t\t\t\t\tcase int:\n2095\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", v)\n2096\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &v\n2097\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n2098\t\t\t\t\tcase float64:\n2099\t\t\t\t\t\t// JSON numbers are often parsed as float64\n2100\t\t\t\t\t\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\n2101\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnum := int(v)\n2102\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", num)\n2103\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &num\n2104\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n2105\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2106\t\t\t\t\tcase bool:\n2107\t\t\t\t\t\t// Some APIs use true/false as error codes\n2108\t\t\t\t\t\tif v {\n2109\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"true\"\n2110\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n2111\t\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n2112\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"false\"\n2113\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n2114\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2115\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n2116\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip unsupported types\n2117\t\t\t\t\t}\n2118\t\n2119\t\t\t\t\t// Validate against value patterns if specified\n2120\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched := true\n2121\t\t\t\t\tif len(pattern.ValuePatterns) > 0 {\n2122\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n2123\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, valuePattern := range pattern.ValuePatterns {\n2124\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatched, _ := regexp.MatchString(valuePattern, codeValue)\n2125\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif matched {\n2126\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = true\n2127\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = valuePattern\n2128\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n2129\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2130\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n2131\t\t\t\t\t}\n2132\t\n2133\t\t\t\t\t// Check numeric-only constraint\n2134\t\t\t\t\tif pattern.NumericOnly && numericCode == nil {\n2135\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n2136\t\t\t\t\t}\n2137\t\n2138\t\t\t\t\tif patternsMatched && codeValue != \"\" {\n2139\t\t\t\t\t\tmatches = append(matches, ErrorCodeMatch{\n2140\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFieldName:      fieldName,\n2141\t\t\t\t\t\t\tCodeValue:      codeValue,\n2142\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNumericCode:    numericCode,\n2143\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMatchedPattern: matchedPattern,\n2144\t\t\t\t\t\t\tPosition:       fmt.Sprintf(\"field '%s' in response body\", fieldName),\n2145\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n2146\t\t\t\t\t}\n2147\t\t\t\t}\n2148\t\t\t}\n2149\t\t}\n2150\t\n2151\t\treturn matches\n2152\t}\n2153\t\n2154\t// ValidateErrorCodeInResponse checks if a specific error code is present in a response.\n2155\t// This is a convenience wrapper around FindErrorCodesInResponse for checking a single code.\n2156\t//\n2157\t// Parameters:\n2158\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body\n2159\t//   - expectedCode: The error code value to look for (as string)\n2160\t//   - fieldName: The field name to check (empty string to check all common fields)\n2161\t//\n2162\t// Returns true if the expected error code is found, false otherwise.\n2163\t//\n2164\t// Example usage:\n2165\t//\n2166\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n2167\t//\thasInvalidToken := ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\", \"error\")\n2168\t//\tif hasInvalidToken {\n2169\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Response contains invalid_token error\")\n2170\t//\t}\n2171\tfunc ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(responseBody interface{}, expectedCode string, fieldName string) bool {\n2172\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n2173\t\t\treturn false\n2174\t\t}\n2175\t\n2176\t\tvar pattern EnhancedErrorCodePattern\n2177\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2178\t\t\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n2179\t\t\t\tFieldNames: []string{fieldName},\n2180\t\t\t}\n2181\t\t} else {\n2182\t\t\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n2183\t\t\t\tFieldNames: []string{\"error_code\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"},\n2184\t\t\t}\n2185\t\t}\n2186\t\n2187\t\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody, []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{pattern})\n2188\t\tfor _, match := range matches {\n2189\t\t\tif match.CodeValue == expectedCode {\n2190\t\t\t\treturn true\n2191\t\t\t}\n2192\t\t}\n2193\t\n2194\t\treturn false\n2195\t}\n2196\t\n2197\t// parseIntFromString attempts to parse a string as an integer.\n2198\tfunc parseIntFromString(s string) (int, error) {\n2199\t\tvar result int\n2200\t\t_, err := fmt.Sscanf(s, \"%d\", &result)\n2201\t\treturn result, err\n2202\t}\n2203\t\n2204\t// =============================================================================\n2205\t// STATUS CODE RANGE VALIDATION (PATTERN-BASED)\n2206\t// =============================================================================\n2207\t\n2208\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt validates a status code against a pattern string.\n2209\t// It supports patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx' to check if a status code\n2210\t// falls within a specified range.\n2211\t//\n2212\t// Parameters:\n2213\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n2214\t//   - actual: The actual status code to validate\n2215\t//\n2216\t// Returns error if the pattern is invalid or if the actual code doesn't match the range.\n2217\t// Returns nil if the actual code falls within the specified range.\n2218\t//\n2219\t// Example usage:\n2220\t//\n2221\t//\t// Validate 404 is in 4xx range\n2222\t//\terr := ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"4xx\", 404)\n2223\t//\tif err != nil {\n2224\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %v\", err)\n2225\t//\t}\n2226\t//\n2227\t//\t// Validate 500 is in 5xx range\n2228\t//\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"5xx\", 500)\n2229\t//\n2230\t//\t// This returns an error because 200 is not in 4xx range\n2231\t//\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"4xx\", 200)\n2232\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(pattern string, actual int) error {\n2233\t\t// Validate pattern format\n2234\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n2235\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2236\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n2237\t\t\t\t\"pattern in format 'Nxx' (3 chars)\",\n2238\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%s' (%d chars)\", pattern, len(pattern)),\n2239\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern format: %s\", pattern),\n2240\t\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet\n2241\t\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName\n2242\t\t\t\t\"\", // location\n2243\t\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields\n2244\t\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails\n2245\t\t\t\t\"\", // rangeInfo\n2246\t\t\t\t[]string{\n2247\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern '%s' has invalid length\", pattern),\n2248\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern must be exactly 3 characters (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\",\n2249\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Got %d characters, expected 3\", len(pattern)),\n2250\t\t\t\t},\n2251\t\t\t)\n2252\t\t}\n2253\t\n2254\t\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\n2255\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n2256\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n2257\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2258\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n2259\t\t\t\t\"century digit 1-5\",\n2260\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%c' (ASCII %d)\", centuryChar, centuryChar),\n2261\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern century in '%s'\", pattern),\n2262\t\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet\n2263\t\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName\n2264\t\t\t\t\"\", // location\n2265\t\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields\n2266\t\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails\n2267\t\t\t\t\"\", // rangeInfo\n2268\t\t\t\t[]string{\n2269\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern '%s' has invalid century digit\", pattern),\n2270\t\t\t\t\t\"Valid century digits: 1 (1xx), 2 (2xx), 3 (3xx), 4 (4xx), 5 (5xx)\",\n2271\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Got '%c', expected digit between '1' and '5'\", centuryChar),\n2272\t\t\t\t},\n2273\t\t\t)\n2274\t\t}\n2275\t\n2276\t\t// Validate 'xx' suffix\n2277\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n2278\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2279\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n2280\t\t\t\t\"pattern ending with 'xx'\",\n2281\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", pattern[1:]),\n2282\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern suffix in '%s'\", pattern),\n2283\t\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet\n2284\t\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName\n2285\t\t\t\t\"\", // location\n2286\t\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields\n2287\t\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails\n2288\t\t\t\t\"\", // rangeInfo\n2289\t\t\t\t[]string{\n2290\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern '%s' has invalid suffix\", pattern),\n2291\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern must end with 'xx' (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\",\n2292\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Got '%s', expected 'xx'\", pattern[1:]),\n2293\t\t\t\t},\n2294\t\t\t)\n2295\t\t}\n2296\t\n2297\t\t// Calculate the range from the pattern\n2298\t\tcentury := int(centuryChar - '0')\n2299\t\tminRange := century * 100\n2300\t\tmaxRange := minRange + 99\n2301\t\n2302\t\t// Get description for the range\n2303\t\tdesc, _ := GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n2304\t\n2305\t\t// Check if actual code is within the range\n2306\t\tif actual < minRange || actual > maxRange {\n2307\t\t\texpectedRange := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d-%d)\", pattern, minRange, maxRange)\n2308\t\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n2309\t\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s (%d-%d)\", pattern, desc, minRange, maxRange)\n2310\t\t\t}\n2311\t\n2312\t\t\t// Calculate distance from valid range\n2313\t\t\tvar distanceInfo string\n2314\t\t\tvar validationDetails []string\n2315\t\n2316\t\t\tif actual < minRange {\n2317\t\t\t\tdistance := minRange - actual\n2318\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes below range\", distance)\n2319\t\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails,\n2320\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Expected range: %d-%d (%s)\", minRange, maxRange, desc),\n2321\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Actual status code: %d\", actual),\n2322\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Distance from valid range: %s\", distanceInfo),\n2323\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern context: %s\", pattern),\n2324\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes below the minimum %d\", actual, distance, minRange))\n2325\t\t\t} else {\n2326\t\t\t\tdistance := actual - maxRange\n2327\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes above range\", distance)\n2328\t\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails,\n2329\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Expected range: %d-%d (%s)\", minRange, maxRange, desc),\n2330\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Actual status code: %d\", actual),\n2331\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Distance from valid range: %s\", distanceInfo),\n2332\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern context: %s\", pattern),\n2333\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes above the maximum %d\", actual, distance, maxRange))\n2334\t\t\t}\n2335\t\n2336\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2337\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n2338\t\t\t\texpectedRange,\n2339\t\t\t\tactual,\n2340\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"status code validation failed for pattern '%s'\", pattern),\n2341\t\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n2342\t\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n2343\t\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n2344\t\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n2345\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern: %s, Distance: %s\", pattern, distanceInfo),\n2346\t\t\t\texpectedRange,\n2347\t\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n2348\t\t\t)\n2349\t\t}\n2350\t\n2351\t\treturn nil\n2352\t}\n2353\t\n2354\t// ParseStatusCodeRange extracts the century digit and range from a pattern string.\n2355\t// This is a helper function for advanced use cases where you need the range bounds.\n2356\t//\n2357\t// Parameters:\n2358\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n2359\t//\n2360\t// Returns the min and max values of the range, or an error if the pattern is invalid.\n2361\t//\n2362\t// Example usage:\n2363\t//\n2364\t//\tmin, max, err := ParseStatusCodeRange(\"4xx\")\n2365\t//\tif err != nil {\n2366\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Invalid pattern: %v\", err)\n2367\t//\t} else {\n2368\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Range: %d-%d\\n\", min, max)\n2369\t//\t}\n2370\tfunc ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern string) (min int, max int, err error) {\n2371\t\t// Validate pattern format\n2372\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n2373\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern format: %s (expected format: '4xx', '5xx', etc.)\", pattern)\n2374\t\t}\n2375\t\n2376\t\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\n2377\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n2378\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n2379\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern century: %c (must be 1-5)\", centuryChar)\n2380\t\t}\n2381\t\n2382\t\t// Validate 'xx' suffix\n2383\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n2384\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern suffix: %s (expected 'xx')\", pattern)\n2385\t\t}\n2386\t\n2387\t\t// Calculate the range from the pattern\n2388\t\tcentury := int(centuryChar - '0')\n2389\t\tminRange := century * 100\n2390\t\tmaxRange := minRange + 99\n2391\t\n2392\t\treturn minRange, maxRange, nil\n2393\t}\n2394\t\n2395\t// GetStatusCodeRangeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code range pattern.\n2396\t//\n2397\t// Parameters:\n2398\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n2399\t//\n2400\t// Returns a description string or an error if the pattern is invalid.\n2401\t//\n2402\t// Example usage:\n2403\t//\n2404\t//\tdesc, err := GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(\"4xx\")\n2405\t//\tif err != nil {\n2406\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Invalid pattern: %v\", err)\n2407\t//\t} else {\n2408\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Description: %s\\n\", desc)\n2409\t//\t}\n2410\tfunc GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern string) (string, error) {\n2411\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n2412\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern format: %s\", pattern)\n2413\t\t}\n2414\t\n2415\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n2416\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n2417\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern century: %c\", centuryChar)\n2418\t\t}\n2419\t\n2420\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n2421\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern suffix: %s\", pattern)\n2422\t\t}\n2423\t\n2424\t\tdescriptions := map[byte]string{\n2425\t\t\t'1': \"Informational\",\n2426\t\t\t'2': \"Success\",\n2427\t\t\t'3': \"Redirection\",\n2428\t\t\t'4': \"Client Error\",\n2429\t\t\t'5': \"Server Error\",\n2430\t\t}\n2431\t\n2432\t\tdesc, ok := descriptions[centuryChar]\n2433\t\tif !ok {\n2434\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"unknown century: %c\", centuryChar)\n2435\t\t}\n2436\t\n2437\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", desc, pattern), nil\n2438\t}\n2439\t\n2440\t// =============================================================================\n2441\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS FOR CREATING VALIDATION ERRORS\n2442\t// =============================================================================\n2443\t\n2444\t// FormatValidationError creates a standardized ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n2445\t//\n2446\t// This helper function automatically generates relevant suggestions based on the\n2447\t// validation type and the mismatch between expected and actual values.\n2448\t//\n2449\t// Parameters:\n2450\t//   - validationType: The type of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\")\n2451\t//   - expected: The expected value\n2452\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n2453\t//   - context: Additional context (optional)\n2454\t//   - responseSnippet: Excerpt from response (optional)\n2455\t//   - customSuggestions: Optional custom suggestions (if provided, these override auto-generated suggestions)\n2456\t//\n2457\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n2458\t//\n2459\t// Example usage with auto-generated suggestions:\n2460\t//\n2461\t//\terr := FormatValidationError(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", \"\")\n2462\t//\n2463\t// Example usage with custom suggestions:\n2464\t//\n2465\t//\terr := FormatValidationError(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", \"\",\n2466\t//\t    \"Check the endpoint documentation\", \"Verify the API version\")\n2467\tfunc FormatValidationError(validationType string, expected, actual interface{}, context, responseSnippet string, customSuggestions ...string) ValidationError {\n2468\t\tsuggestions := customSuggestions\n2469\t\tif len(customSuggestions) == 0 {\n2470\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(validationType, expected, actual)\n2471\t\t}\n2472\t\n2473\t\tve := ValidationError{\n2474\t\t\tErrorType:        validationType,\n2475\t\t\tMessage:          generateMessageFromParts(validationType, expected, actual),\n2476\t\t\tExpected:         expected,\n2477\t\t\tActual:           actual,\n2478\t\t\tContext:          context,\n2479\t\t\tResponseSnippet:  responseSnippet,\n2480\t\t\tSuggestions:      suggestions,\n2481\t\t}\n2482\t\n2483\t\treturn ve\n2484\t}\n2485\t\n2486\t// FormatValidationErrorWithDetails creates a ValidationError with extended field support.\n2487\t//\n2488\t// This function provides full customization over all ValidationError fields, including\n2489\t// optional fields like FieldName, Location, RelatedFields, PatternDetails, RangeInfo, ValidationDetails, and custom Suggestions.\n2490\t// Use this when you need more detailed error information than FormatValidationError provides.\n2491\t//\n2492\t// Parameters:\n2493\t//   - validationType: The type of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n2494\t//   - expected: The expected value\n2495\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n2496\t//   - context: Additional context about the validation operation\n2497\t//   - responseSnippet: Excerpt from the response for debugging\n2498\t//   - fieldName: The specific field name where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n2499\t//   - location: Where in the input the error occurred (e.g., \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\")\n2500\t//   - relatedFields: List of fields related to this error (e.g., [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"])\n2501\t//   - patternDetails: Information about pattern matching failures\n2502\t//   - rangeInfo: Range boundaries for range validation failures (e.g., \"400-499 (Client Error)\")\n2503\t//   - validationDetails: Additional validation-specific details as a list of strings\n2504\t//   - customSuggestions: Optional custom suggestions (if provided, these override auto-generated suggestions)\n2505\t//\n2506\t// Returns a ValidationError with all specified fields populated.\n2507\t//\n2508\t// Example usage with auto-generated suggestions:\n2509\t//\n2510\t//\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2511\t//\t    \"error_message\",\n2512\t//\t    \"invalid.*token\",\n2513\t//\t    \"access_denied\",\n2514\t//\t    \"OAuth validation\",\n2515\t//\t    `{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`,\n2516\t//\t    \"error\",\n2517\t//\t    \"field 'user.access_token'\",\n2518\t//\t    []string{\"user.access_token\", \"user.refresh_token\"},\n2519\t//\t    \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\",\n2520\t//\t    \"\",\n2521\t//\t    []string{\"No matching error field found\", \"Checked 3 error message fields\"},\n2522\t//\t)\n2523\t//\n2524\t// Example usage with custom suggestions:\n2525\t//\n2526\t//\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2527\t//\t    \"error_message\",\n2528\t//\t    \"invalid.*token\",\n2529\t//\t    \"access_denied\",\n2530\t//\t    \"OAuth validation\",\n2531\t//\t    `{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`,\n2532\t//\t    \"error\",\n2533\t//\t    \"line 42 in user configuration\",\n2534\t//\t    []string{\"token_type\", \"access_token\"},\n2535\t//\t    \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\",\n2536\t//\t    \"\",\n2537\t//\t    []string{\"No matching error field found\"},\n2538\t//\t    \"Check token format\", \"Verify OAuth scopes\",\n2539\t//\t)\n2540\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2541\t\tvalidationType string,\n2542\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n2543\t\tcontext, responseSnippet string,\n2544\t\tfieldName, location string,\n2545\t\trelatedFields []string,\n2546\t\tpatternDetails, rangeInfo string,\n2547\t\tvalidationDetails []string,\n2548\t\tcustomSuggestions ...string,\n2549\t) ValidationError {\n2550\t\tsuggestions := customSuggestions\n2551\t\tif len(customSuggestions) == 0 {\n2552\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(validationType, expected, actual)\n2553\t\t}\n2554\t\n2555\t\tve := ValidationError{\n2556\t\t\tErrorType:         validationType,\n2557\t\t\tMessage:           generateMessageFromParts(validationType, expected, actual),\n2558\t\t\tExpected:          expected,\n2559\t\t\tActual:            actual,\n2560\t\t\tContext:           context,\n2561\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   responseSnippet,\n2562\t\t\tFieldName:         fieldName,\n2563\t\t\tLocation:          location,\n2564\t\t\tRelatedFields:     relatedFields,\n2565\t\t\tPatternDetails:    patternDetails,\n2566\t\t\tRangeInfo:         rangeInfo,\n2567\t\t\tValidationDetails: validationDetails,\n2568\t\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n2569\t\t}\n2570\t\n2571\t\treturn ve\n2572\t}\n2573\t\n2574\t// generateMessageFromParts creates a concise human-readable message from validation details.\n2575\tfunc generateMessageFromParts(validationType string, expected, actual interface{}) string {\n2576\t\t// Start with the basic validation failure message\n2577\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", validationType)\n2578\t\n2579\t\t// Add expected vs actual information if available\n2580\t\tif expected != nil || actual != nil {\n2581\t\t\tmsg += \": expected \"\n2582\t\t\tswitch exp := expected.(type) {\n2583\t\t\tcase int:\n2584\t\t\t\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", exp)\n2585\t\t\tcase string:\n2586\t\t\t\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n2587\t\t\tcase []int:\n2588\t\t\t\tmsg += \"one of [\"\n2589\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n2590\t\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n2591\t\t\t\t\t\tmsg += \", \"\n2592\t\t\t\t\t}\n2593\t\t\t\t\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", code)\n2594\t\t\t\t}\n2595\t\t\t\tmsg += \"]\"\n2596\t\t\tdefault:\n2597\t\t\t\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n2598\t\t\t}\n2599\t\n2600\t\t\tmsg += \", got \"\n2601\t\t\tswitch act := actual.(type) {\n2602\t\t\tcase int:\n2603\t\t\t\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", act)\n2604\t\t\tcase string:\n2605\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 50 {\n2606\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:50] + \"...\"\n2607\t\t\t\t}\n2608\t\t\t\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n2609\t\t\tdefault:\n2610\t\t\t\tmsg += fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n2611\t\t\t}\n2612\t\t}\n2613\t\n2614\t\treturn msg\n2615\t}\n2616\t\n2617\t// generateSuggestions generates relevant suggestions based on validation type and values.\n2618\tfunc generateSuggestions(validationType string, expected, actual interface{}) []string {\n2619\t\tvar suggestions []string\n2620\t\n2621\t\tswitch validationType {\n2622\t\tcase \"status_code\":\n2623\t\t\tsuggestions = generateStatusCodeSuggestions(expected, actual)\n2624\t\tcase \"error_message\":\n2625\t\t\tsuggestions = generateErrorMessageSuggestions(expected, actual)\n2626\t\tcase \"content_type\":\n2627\t\t\tsuggestions = generateContentTypeSuggestions(expected, actual)\n2628\t\tcase \"status_code_range\":\n2629\t\t\tsuggestions = generateStatusCodeRangeSuggestions(expected, actual)\n2630\t\tdefault:\n2631\t\t\tsuggestions = []string{\"Review the request parameters and try again\"}\n2632\t\t}\n2633\t\n2634\t\treturn suggestions\n2635\t}\n2636\t\n2637\t// generateStatusCodeSuggestions generates suggestions for status code mismatches.\n2638\tfunc generateStatusCodeSuggestions(expected, actual interface{}) []string {\n2639\t\tactualCode, ok := actual.(int)\n2640\t\tif !ok {\n2641\t\t\treturn []string{\"Verify the response format is correct\"}\n2642\t\t}\n2643\t\n2644\t\t// Use the specific suggestions function which provides detailed suggestions per code\n2645\t\tsuggestions := getStatusCodeSpecificSuggestions(actualCode)\n2646\t\n2647\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n2648\t\t\t// Fallback to generic suggestions if no specific ones found\n2649\t\t\tif actualCode >= 400 && actualCode < 500 {\n2650\t\t\t\treturn []string{\n2651\t\t\t\t\t\"Review request parameters and body\",\n2652\t\t\t\t\t\"Check API documentation for requirements\",\n2653\t\t\t\t\t\"Verify all headers are correctly set\",\n2654\t\t\t\t\t\"Ensure request complies with API contracts\",\n2655\t\t\t\t}\n2656\t\t\t}\n2657\t\t\tif actualCode >= 500 && actualCode < 600 {\n2658\t\t\t\treturn []string{\n2659\t\t\t\t\t\"Implement retry logic with exponential backoff\",\n2660\t\t\t\t\t\"Check service status page for ongoing issues\",\n2661\t\t\t\t\t\"Contact support if the issue persists\",\n2662\t\t\t\t\t\"Review server logs if accessible\",\n2663\t\t\t\t}\n2664\t\t\t}\n2665\t\t\treturn []string{\"Review the request and response for details\"}\n2666\t\t}\n2667\t\n2668\t\treturn suggestions\n2669\t}\n2670\t\n2671\t// generateErrorMessageSuggestions generates suggestions for error message mismatches.\n2672\tfunc generateErrorMessageSuggestions(expected, actual interface{}) []string {\n2673\t\texpectedStr, _ := expected.(string)\n2674\t\tactualStr, _ := actual.(string)\n2675\t\n2676\t\texpectedLower := strings.ToLower(expectedStr)\n2677\t\tactualLower := strings.ToLower(actualStr)\n2678\t\n2679\t\tvar suggestions []string\n2680\t\n2681\t\t// Check for common error patterns\n2682\t\tswitch {\n2683\t\tcase strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"token\"):\n2684\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n2685\t\t\t\t\"Verify token is correctly formatted\",\n2686\t\t\t\t\"Check token is for the refresh\")\n2693\t\t\t}\n2694\t\tcase strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"auth\") && strings.Contains(actualLower, \"denied\"):\n2695\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n2696\t\t\t\t\"Verify account permissions\",\n2697\t\t\t\t\"Check if additional authorization scopes are needed\",\n2698\t\t\t\t\"Review API key or token permissions\")\n2699\t\tcase strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"not found\") || strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"does not exist\"):\n2700\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n2701\t\t\t\t\"Verify the resource identifier is correct\",\n2702\t\t\t\t\"Check if the resource exists\",\n2703\t\t\t\t\"Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted\")\n2704\t\tcase strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"validation\") || strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"invalid\"):\n2705\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n2706\t\t\t\t\"Review request body for missing required fields\",\n2707\t\t\t\t\"Verify data types match expected format\",\n2708\t\t\t\t\"Check field constraints (length, format, values)\")\n2709\t\tcase strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"rate\") || strings.Contains(expectedLower, \"quota\"):\n2710\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n2711\t\t\t\t\"Implement rate limiting\",\n2712\t\t\t\t\"Check API quota limits\",\n2713\t\t\t\t\"Add exponential backoff for retries\")\n2714\t\tdefault:\n2715\t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions,\n2716\t\t\t\t\"Review the error message for specific details\",\n2717\t\t\t\t\"Check API documentation for this error type\",\n2718\t\t\t\t\"Verify request parameters match requirements\")\n2719\t\t}\n2720\t\n2721\t\treturn suggestions\n2722\t}\n2723\t\n2724\t// generateContentTypeSuggestions generates suggestions for content-type mismatches.\n2725\tfunc generateContentTypeSuggestions(expected, actual interface{}) []string {\n2726\t\treturn []string{\n2727\t\t\t\"Verify Content-Type header matches request body  - Consider expanding pattern to include 'auth' or 'access' related terms\n2796\t//\n2797\t// Example enhanced error output for substring pattern mismatch:\n2798\t//\n2799\t//\tresponse := Package validate provides helper functions for validating HTTP responses and other data.\npackage validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"regexp\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsValid checks if an HTTP response's status code matches one or more expected codes.\n// It supports both a single status code and a slice of valid status codes, making it useful for\n// validating API responses where multiple status codes might indicate success (e.g., 200 OK and 206 Partial Content).\n//\n// The expected parameter can be:\n//   - A single int (e.g., 200)\n//   - A slice of ints (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code matches any of the expected codes, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tsingleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 200)\n//\tmultipleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, []int{200, 201, 204})\n//\tnoContentCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 204)\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tstatusCode := resp.StatusCode\n\n\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n\tcase int:\n\t\treturn statusCode == v\n\tcase []int:\n\t\tfor _, code := range v {\n\t\t\tif statusCode == code {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn false\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates an error (4xx or 5xx).\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-599 range, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsError(response) {\n//\t    // Handle error response\n//\t}\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n}\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a client error (4xx).\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-499 range, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(response) {\n//\t    // Handle 4xx client error (bad request, unauthorized, etc.)\n//\t}\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500\n}\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a server error (5xx).\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code is in the 500-599 range, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(response) {\n//\t    // Handle 5xx server error (internal server error, bad gateway, etc.)\n//\t}\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n}\n\n// ContentTypeIsValid checks if a response's Content-Type header matches the expected pattern.\n// It supports pattern matching where the base content-type matches regardless of parameters.\n// For example, \"application/json\" will match \"application/json; charset=utf-8\".\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - expected: The expected content-type pattern (e.g., \"application/json\")\n//\n// Returns true if the response Content-Type matches the expected pattern, false otherwise.\n// Returns false if the response is nil or has no Content-Type header.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif ContentTypeIsValid(response, \"application/json\") {\n//\t    // Handle JSON response\n//\t}\nfunc ContentTypeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected string) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tactual := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif actual == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse both content-type strings to extract base media types\n\tactualMediaType := parseContentType(actual)\n\texpectedMediaType := parseContentType(expected)\n\n\treturn actualMediaType == expectedMediaType\n}\n\n// parseContentType extracts the base media type from a content-type string.\n// It strips parameters like charset, boundary, etc.\n// For example, \"application/json; charset=utf-8\" becomes \"application/json\".\nfunc parseContentType(contentType string) string {\n\tif contentType == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Split by semicolon to separate media type from parameters\n\t// The first part before any semicolon is the base media type\n\tfor i, c := range contentType {\n\t\tif c == ';' {\n\t\t\treturn trimSpace(contentType[:i])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// No parameters found, return the whole string trimmed\n\treturn trimSpace(contentType)\n}\n\n// trimSpace removes leading and trailing whitespace from a string.\nfunc trimSpace(s string) string {\n\tstart := 0\n\tend := len(s)\n\n\tfor start < end && (s[start] == ' ' || s[start] == '\\t' || s[start] == '\\n' || s[start] == '\\r') {\n\t\tstart++\n\t}\n\n\tfor end > start && (s[end-1] == ' ' || s[end-1] == '\\t' || s[end-1] == '\\n' || s[end-1] == '\\r') {\n\t\tend--\n\t}\n\n\treturn s[start:end]\n}\n\n// ErrorResponseFieldNames specifies custom field names to check when validating error response structures.\n// If nil, the default field names \"error\" and \"message\" are checked.\ntype ErrorResponseFieldNames struct {\n\t// PrimaryFieldName is the main error field to check (e.g., \"error\", \"detail\", \"error_description\")\n\tPrimaryFieldName string\n\t// SecondaryFieldName is an optional secondary field to check (e.g., \"message\", \"description\")\n\tSecondaryFieldName string\n}\n\n// DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames returns the default field names used for error response validation.\n// This checks for both \"error\" and \"message\" fields.\nfunc DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames() *ErrorResponseFieldNames {\n\treturn &ErrorResponseFieldNames{\n\t\tPrimaryFieldName:   \"error\",\n\t\tSecondaryFieldName: \"message\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorResponseStructureIsValid validates whether a response object contains a valid error response structure.\n// It checks for the presence of common error fields and returns whether the structure is valid.\n//\n// The response parameter can be:\n//   - map[string]interface{}: A parsed JSON object representing the response body\n//   - Any type that can be inspected for error fields using reflection\n//\n// The fieldNames parameter allows customization of which fields to check:\n//   - If nil, checks for default fields \"error\" and \"message\"\n//   - If provided, checks for the specified primary and secondary field names\n//\n// Returns true if the response contains at least one of the expected error fields with a non-empty value,\n// false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Using default field names (checks for \"error\" and \"message\")\n//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"Invalid input\"}\n//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, nil)\n//\n//\t// Using custom field names\n//\tcustomFields := &ErrorResponseFieldNames{PrimaryFieldName: \"detail\", SecondaryFieldName: \"description\"}\n//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"detail\": \"Resource not found\"}\n//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, customFields)\nfunc ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(response interface{}, fieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames) bool {\n\tif response == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Use default field names if not provided\n\tif fieldNames == nil {\n\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to convert to map for field checking\n\trespMap, ok := response.(map[string]interface{})\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check primary field\n\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\n\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check secondary field\n\tif fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\n\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// isNonEmptyValue checks if a value is non-empty and meaningful.\n// Returns true for strings with content, numbers (non-zero), and true booleans.\n// Returns false for empty strings, zero values, false booleans, and nil.\nfunc isNonEmptyValue(value interface{}) bool {\n\tif value == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n\tcase string:\n\t\treturn v != \"\"\n\tcase int:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase int8:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase int16:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase int32:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase int64:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint8:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint16:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint32:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase uint64:\n\t\treturn v != 0\n\tcase float32:\n\t\treturn v != 0.0\n\tcase float64:\n\t\treturn v != 0.0\n\tcase bool:\n\t\treturn v\n\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n}\n\n// CORSConfig specifies expected CORS header values for validation.\ntype CORSConfig struct {\n\t// AllowOrigin is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Origin.\n\t// Use \"*\" for wildcard CORS, or specify an exact origin like \"https://example.com\".\n\t// If empty, the origin header is not validated.\n\tAllowOrigin string\n\n\t// AllowMethods is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.\n\t// If empty, the methods header is not validated.\n\tAllowMethods string\n\n\t// AllowHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.\n\t// If empty, the headers header is not validated.\n\tAllowHeaders string\n\n\t// AllowCredentials is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Credentials.\n\t// If true, expects \"true\"; if false, expects \"true\" or header not present.\n\t// This is typically used with specific origins (not wildcard).\n\tAllowCredentials bool\n\n\t// ExposeHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Expose-Headers.\n\t// If empty, the expose headers header is not validated.\n\tExposeHeaders string\n\n\t// MaxAge is the expected value for Access-Control-Max-Age.\n\t// If empty, the max-age header is not validated.\n\tMaxAge string\n}\n\n// CORSHeadersIsValid validates CORS headers on an HTTP response, particularly for error responses.\n// It checks the presence and values of common CORS headers and returns whether they match the expected configuration.\n//\n// This function is particularly useful for validating that error responses (4xx/5xx) include proper CORS headers,\n// which is required for browsers to receive and process error responses from cross-origin requests.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - config: Expected CORS configuration (use nil for basic validation that only checks header presence)\n//\n// Returns true if all specified CORS headers are present and match expected values, false otherwise.\n// Returns false if the response is nil.\n//\n// When config is nil, only validates that Access-Control-Allow-Origin header exists (non-empty).\n// When config is provided, validates only the fields that are non-empty in the config.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Basic validation - check if CORS headers exist\n//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, nil) {\n//\t    // CORS headers are present\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Validate specific origin\n//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\"}\n//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n//\t    // CORS headers match expected origin\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Validate wildcard CORS\n//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"*\", AllowCredentials: false}\n//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n//\t    // Wildcard CORS is properly configured\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Validate full CORS configuration\n//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{\n//\t    AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\",\n//\t    AllowMethods: \"GET, POST, OPTIONS\",\n//\t    AllowHeaders: \"Content-Type, Authorization\",\n//\t    AllowCredentials: true,\n//\t}\n//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n//\t    // Full CORS configuration is valid\n//\t}\nfunc CORSHeadersIsValid(resp *http.Response, config *CORSConfig) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// If no config provided, do basic validation: check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin exists\n\tif config == nil {\n\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\treturn origin != \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Origin if specified\n\tif config.AllowOrigin != \"\" {\n\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for wildcard or exact match\n\t\tif config.AllowOrigin == \"*\" {\n\t\t\t// Wildcard is valid\n\t\t\tif origin != \"*\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Exact origin match (case-sensitive for origins)\n\t\t\tif origin != config.AllowOrigin {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Methods if specified\n\tif config.AllowMethods != \"\" {\n\t\tmethods := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\t\tif methods != config.AllowMethods {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Headers if specified\n\tif config.AllowHeaders != \"\" {\n\t\theaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\t\tif headers != config.AllowHeaders {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Credentials if specified\n\tif config.AllowCredentials {\n\t\tcredentials := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials\")\n\t\tif credentials != \"true\" {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Expose-Headers if specified\n\tif config.ExposeHeaders != \"\" {\n\t\texposeHeaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Expose-Headers\")\n\t\tif exposeHeaders != config.ExposeHeaders {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Access-Control-Max-Age if specified\n\tif config.MaxAge != \"\" {\n\t\tmaxAge := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Max-Age\")\n\t\tif maxAge != config.MaxAge {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE PATTERN VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorMessagePattern specifies pattern matching configuration for error messages.\ntype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\n\t// Pattern is the regex pattern to match against error messages\n\tPattern string\n\n\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether matching should be case-insensitive\n\tCaseInsensitive bool\n\n\t// MatchAny indicates whether to match any field (true) or all fields (false)\n\tMatchAny bool\n\n\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\")\n\t// If empty, checks default fields: \"error\", \"message\"\n\tFieldNames []string\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessagePattern validates error message content against regex patterns.\n//\n// This function extracts error messages from response bodies and validates them\n// against a regex pattern. It supports both exact matching and case-insensitive\n// pattern matching for flexible error validation.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n//   - pattern: Regex pattern to match against error messages\n//   - caseInsensitive: If true, performs case-insensitive matching\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - true if the error message matches the pattern, false otherwise\n//   - error if JSON parsing fails or response is invalid\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Check for \"not found\" error (case-insensitive)\n//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `(?i)not found`, false)\n//\n//\t// Check for \"invalid.*token\" pattern\n//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `invalid.*token`, true)\nfunc ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes []byte, pattern string, caseInsensitive bool) (bool, error) {\n\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n\t}\n\n\tif pattern == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse JSON body\n\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Compile regex pattern\n\tregexPattern := pattern\n\tif caseInsensitive {\n\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + pattern\n\t}\n\n\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check default error fields\n\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessage validates error message content against a pattern.\n//\n// This function checks if a response body contains an error message that matches\n// the expected pattern. It supports both regex patterns and simple substring matching.\n// When the pattern is found, it returns nil. When the pattern is not found, it returns\n// a descriptive error including a snippet of the actual response.\n//\n// The function automatically detects whether the pattern is a regex or substring:\n// - If the pattern contains regex metacharacters (. * + ? ^ $ { } [ ] ( ) | \\), it's treated as regex\n// - Otherwise, it's treated as a simple substring search (case-sensitive)\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - response: Raw response body bytes\n//   - expectedPattern: Pattern to search for (regex or substring)\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - nil if the pattern is found in the response\n//   - error if the pattern is not found or if response is invalid\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Regex pattern matching\n//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*token\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    // Pattern not found\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Simple substring matching\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"not found\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    // Substring not found\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Common error patterns\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"unauthorized\")        // OAuth errors\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*credentials\") // Auth errors\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"rate.*limit\")          // Rate limiting\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"timeout\")               // Timeout errors\n//\n// # Common Error Pattern Examples\n//\n// The function auto-detects regex vs substring matching:\n//\n// # OAuth 2.0 Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"error_description\": \"The access token expired\"}`)\n//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid_token\")  // substring match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"access_denied|invalid_grant\")  // regex match\n//\n// # Authentication Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Authentication failed: invalid credentials\"}`)\n//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"authentication.*failed\")  // regex match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Invalid username or password\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*credentials\")  // regex match\n//\n// # Authorization Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Permission denied\"}`)\n//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"permission.*denied\")  // regex match\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"User is not authorized to access this resource\"}`)\n//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not.*authorized\")  // regex match\n//\n// # Validation Errors\n//\n//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Validation failed: required field missing\"}`)\n//\terr =
