{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_6a3e4a3f9d004baaa5444a2a","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage validate\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"strings\"\n6\t\t\"sync\"\n7\t)\n8\t\n9\t// ValidationFormatter provides a simplified, fluent API for formatting validation errors consistently.\n10\t//\n11\t// This builder wraps the ValidationError functionality with an ergonomic interface that allows\n12\t// you to construct validation errors step-by-step using method chaining.\n13\t//\n14\t// # Design Pattern\n15\t//\n16\t// ValidationFormatter uses the builder pattern to provide:\n17\t//   - Fluent, chainable methods for readable code\n18\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions when not manually specified\n19\t//   - Type-safe error construction\n20\t//   - Consistent error format across all validation types\n21\t//\n22\t// # Required Fields\n23\t//\n24\t// Only validationType is required (via NewValidationFormatter). All other fields are optional.\n25\t// If suggestions are not provided via WithSuggestions, they will be auto-generated based on\n26\t// the validation type and expected/actual values.\n27\t//\n28\t// # Common Usage Pattern\n29\t//\n30\t// The typical workflow is:\n31\t//  1. Create formatter with NewValidationFormatter(type)\n32\t//  2. Chain With* methods to set optional fields\n33\t//  3. Call Format() to generate the ValidationError\n34\t//\n35\t// # Example\n36\t//\n37\t//\t// Basic usage with auto-generated suggestions\n38\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n39\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n40\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n41\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n42\t//\t    Format()\n43\t//\n44\t//\t// With custom suggestions\n45\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field\").\n46\t//\t    WithExpected(\"required\").\n47\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n48\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"email\").\n49\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Email is required\", \"Check user input\").\n50\t//\t    Format()\n51\t//\n52\t// # Extending for New Validation Types\n53\t//\n54\t// To add support for a new validation type:\n55\t//  1. Use NewValidationFormatter(\"your_type\") with a descriptive type name\n56\t//  2. Set appropriate fields using With* methods\n57\t//  3. Optionally provide custom suggestions via WithSuggestions\n58\t//  4. The formatter will automatically include the type in error output\n59\t//\n60\t// # Fields\n61\t//\n62\t// All fields are private to ensure the builder pattern is used correctly.\n63\t// Access them through the public With* methods and Format().\n64\ttype ValidationFormatter struct {\n65\t\tvalidationType string\n66\t\texpected       interface{}\n67\t\tactual         interface{}\n68\t\tcontext        string\n69\t\tresponseSnippet string\n70\t\tfieldName      string\n71\t\tpatternDetails string\n72\t\trangeInfo      string\n73\t\tvalidationDetails []string\n74\t\tcustomSuggestions []string\n75\t}\n76\t\n77\t// NewValidationFormatter creates a new ValidationFormatter for the given validation type.\n78\t//\n79\t// This is the entry point for the builder pattern. The validationType identifies what\n80\t// kind of validation was performed and is used to:\n81\t//   - Categorize the error in the error message\n82\t//   - Generate appropriate default suggestions\n83\t//   - Provide context for error handling\n84\t//\n85\t// # Parameters\n86\t//\n87\t//   - validationType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n88\t//\n89\t//     Common validation types include:\n90\t//     - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n91\t//     - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n92\t//     - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n93\t//     - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n94\t//     - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n95\t//     - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n96\t//     - Custom types for domain-specific validation\n97\t//\n98\t// # Returns\n99\t//\n100\t// A pointer to a new ValidationFormatter instance ready for method chaining.\n101\t//\n102\t// # Example Usage\n103\t//\n104\t//\t// Basic status code validation\n105\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n106\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n107\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n108\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n109\t//\t    Format()\n110\t//\n111\t//\t// Content-Type validation with context\n112\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n113\t//\t    WithExpected(\"application/json\").\n114\t//\t    WithActual(\"text/html\").\n115\t//\t    WithContext(\"API response\").\n116\t//\t    Format()\n117\t//\n118\t//\t// Custom validation type with all options\n119\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field_check\").\n120\t//\t    WithExpected(\"non_empty\").\n121\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n122\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"username\").\n123\t//\t    WithContext(\"User creation\").\n124\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Username cannot be empty\", \"Must be 3-20 characters\").\n125\t//\t    Format()\n126\tfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n127\t\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n128\t\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n129\t\t}\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\t// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\n133\t//\n134\t// This method specifies what value was expected during the validation check.\n135\t// The expected value is compared against the actual value to provide clear\n136\t// error messages showing what went wrong.\n137\t//\n138\t// # Parameters\n139\t//\n140\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n141\t//\n142\t//     Common types:\n143\t//     - int: Single expected value (e.g., expected status code 200)\n144\t//     - []int: Multiple acceptable values (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n145\t//     - string: Expected pattern or literal value (e.g., \"application/json\")\n146\t//\n147\t// # Returns\n148\t//\n149\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n150\t//\n151\t// # Example\n152\t//\n153\t//\t// Single expected value\n154\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(200)\n155\t//\n156\t//\t// Multiple acceptable values\n157\t//\tformatter.WithExpected([]int{200, 201, 204})\n158\t//\n159\t//\t// String pattern\n160\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(\"application/json\")\n161\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n162\t\tvf.expected = expected\n163\t\treturn vf\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\t// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\n167\t//\n168\t// This method specifies what value was actually received during the validation check.\n169\t// The actual value is compared against the expected value to show the discrepancy\n170\t// that caused the validation failure.\n171\t//\n172\t// # Parameters\n173\t//\n174\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type: int, string, etc.)\n175\t//\n176\t//     Common types:\n177\t//     - int: Actual numeric value (e.g., actual status code 404)\n178\t//     - string: Actual message or content received\n179\t//     - Other types as appropriate for the validation context\n180\t//\n181\t// # Returns\n182\t//\n183\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n184\t//\n185\t// # Example\n186\t//\n187\t//\t// Status code that was received\n188\t//\tformatter.WithActual(404)\n189\t//\n190\t//\t// Error message that was received\n191\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"access denied\")\n192\t//\n193\t//\t// Content-Type that was received\n194\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"text/html\")\n195\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n196\t\tvf.actual = actual\n197\t\treturn vf\n198\t}\n199\t\n200\t// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n201\t//\n202\t// Context provides valuable information about where or when the validation occurred,\n203\t// making errors easier to debug and understand. This is especially useful in logs\n204\t// and test output where multiple validations might be happening.\n205\t//\n206\t// # Parameters\n207\t//\n208\t//   - context: A descriptive string explaining the validation context\n209\t//\n210\t//     Common context examples:\n211\t//     - HTTP endpoints: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n212\t//     - Operation descriptions: \"OAuth token validation\", \"User creation\"\n213\t//     - Test scenarios: \"Positive case test\", \"Negative case test\"\n214\t//     - Component names: \"Authentication service\", \"Database query\"\n215\t//\n216\t// # Returns\n217\t//\n218\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n219\t//\n220\t// # Example\n221\t//\n222\t//\t// HTTP endpoint context\n223\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\")\n224\t//\n225\t//\t// Operation context\n226\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"OAuth token validation\")\n227\t//\n228\t//\t// Test scenario context\n229\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"Testing unauthorized access\")\n230\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n231\t\tvf.context = context\n232\t\treturn vf\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\t// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n236\t//\n237\t// Response snippets provide crucial debugging information by showing the actual\n238\t// content that caused the validation failure. This is particularly useful when\n239\t// debugging complex response structures or unexpected error messages.\n240\t//\n241\t// # Parameters\n242\t//\n243\t//   - snippet: A truncated excerpt from the response (recommended: 100-200 characters)\n244\t//\n245\t//     Best practices:\n246\t//     - Keep snippets concise (100-200 characters recommended)\n247\t//     - Include relevant parts that show the problem\n248\t//     - Truncate long responses with \"...\" to keep errors readable\n249\t//     - Sanitize sensitive data before including in snippets\n250\t//\n251\t// # Returns\n252\t//\n253\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n254\t//\n255\t// # Example\n256\t//\n257\t//\t// Short JSON response snippet\n258\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"code\": \"AUTH_001\"}`)\n259\t//\n260\t//\t// Truncated HTML snippet\n261\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`<html><body>Error: Page not found...</body></html>`)\n262\t//\n263\t//\t// Error message snippet\n264\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(\"Access denied: insufficient permissions for resource\")\n265\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n266\t\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n267\t\treturn vf\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t// WithFieldName sets the specific field name where the error was found.\n271\t//\n272\t// This method identifies the exact field that failed validation, which is especially\n273\t// useful when validating structured data like JSON responses, form fields, or\n274\t// configuration objects.\n275\t//\n276\t// # Parameters\n277\t//\n278\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field where the error occurred\n279\t//\n280\t//     Common examples:\n281\t//     - Response body fields: \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"code\"\n282\t//     - Form fields: \"email\", \"password\", \"username\"\n283\t//     - Nested fields: \"user.email\", \"data.items[0].id\"\n284\t//     - Headers: \"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"\n285\t//\n286\t// # Returns\n287\t//\n288\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n289\t//\n290\t// # Example\n291\t//\n292\t//\t// Simple field name\n293\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"error\")\n294\t//\n295\t//\t// Nested field notation\n296\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"user.email\")\n297\t//\n298\t//\t// Array field notation\n299\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"items[0].id\")\n300\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter {\n301\t\tvf.fieldName = fieldName\n302\t\treturn vf\n303\t}\n304\t\n305\t// WithPatternDetails sets information about pattern matching failures.\n306\t//\n307\t// This method provides additional context when validation involves pattern matching,\n308\t// such as regex validation for error messages, format checking, or content verification.\n309\t// It helps explain what pattern was expected and how the actual value didn't match.\n310\t//\n311\t// # Parameters\n312\t//\n313\t//   - details: Information about the pattern that failed to match\n314\t//\n315\t//     Common examples:\n316\t//     - Regex patterns: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n317\t//     - Format requirements: \"expected format 'user@domain.com'\"\n318\t//     - Pattern descriptions: \"must contain at least one uppercase letter\"\n319\t//     - Match details: \"pattern matched at position 5 but failed validation\"\n320\t//\n321\t// # When to Use\n322\t//\n323\t// Use this method when:\n324\t//   - Validating error messages against regex patterns\n325\t//   - Checking string formats (email, phone, UUID, etc.)\n326\t//   - Verifying content patterns in responses\n327\t//   - Any validation involving pattern matching\n328\t//\n329\t// # Returns\n330\t//\n331\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n332\t//\n333\t// # Example\n334\t//\n335\t//\t// Regex pattern explanation\n336\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\")\n337\t//\n338\t//\t// Format requirement\n339\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"expected format: YYYY-MM-DD\")\n340\t//\n341\t//\t// Content pattern description\n342\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"must contain at least 8 characters\")\n343\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter {\n344\t\tvf.patternDetails = details\n345\t\treturn vf\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// WithRangeInfo sets range boundaries for range validation failures.\n349\t//\n350\t// This method provides context when validating against ranges, such as HTTP status\n351\t// code ranges (4xx, 5xx), numeric ranges, or value intervals. It helps explain the\n352\t// expected boundaries and what range the actual value fell outside of.\n353\t//\n354\t// # Parameters\n355\t//\n356\t//   - info: Description of the range boundaries\n357\t//\n358\t//     Common examples:\n359\t//     - Status code ranges: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n360\t//     - Numeric ranges: \"0-100\", \"1-1000\"\n361\t//     - Descriptive ranges: \"positive numbers only\", \"non-negative integers\"\n362\t//\n363\t// # When to Use\n364\t//\n365\t// Use this method when:\n366\t//   - Validating HTTP status code ranges (e.g., expecting 4xx, got 2xx)\n367\t//   - Checking numeric value ranges (e.g., age 0-120)\n368\t//   - Verifying values within intervals (e.g., percentage 0-100)\n369\t//   - Any validation involving range constraints\n370\t//\n371\t// # Returns\n372\t//\n373\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n374\t//\n375\t// # Example\n376\t//\n377\t//\t// Status code range\n378\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"400-499 (Client Error)\")\n379\t//\n380\t//\t// Numeric range\n381\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"0-120\")\n382\t//\n383\t//\t// Descriptive range\n384\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"positive integers only\")\n385\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\n386\t\tvf.rangeInfo = info\n387\t\treturn vf\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// WithValidationDetails adds additional validation-specific details.\n391\t//\n392\t// This method allows you to provide granular, multi-line information about what was\n393\t// checked during validation and specifically what failed. This is useful for complex\n394\t// validations that check multiple conditions or have detailed requirements.\n395\t//\n396\t// # Parameters\n397\t//\n398\t//   - details: Variable number of detail strings to add to the error\n399\t//\n400\t//     Common uses:\n401\t//     - List specific conditions that failed\n402\t//     - Explain validation rules in detail\n403\t//     - Provide step-by-step validation results\n404\t//     - Document intermediate validation states\n405\t//\n406\t// # When to Use\n407\t//\n408\t// Use this method when:\n409\t//   - Validation involves multiple checks or conditions\n410\t//   - You need to explain complex validation rules\n411\t//   - Detailed debugging information would be helpful\n412\t//   - The validation logic is non-trivial\n413\t//\n414\t// # Returns\n415\t//\n416\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n417\t//\n418\t// # Example\n419\t//\n420\t//\t// Multiple specific failures\n421\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n422\t//\t    \"Password must be at least 8 characters\",\n423\t//\t    \"Password must contain uppercase letters\",\n424\t//\t    \"Password must contain special characters\",\n425\t//\t)\n426\t//\n427\t//\t// Detailed explanation\n428\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n429\t//\t    \"Expected 200-299 status range\",\n430\t//\t    \"Got 500 indicating server error\",\n431\t//\t    \"Server logs show database connection failure\",\n432\t//\t)\n433\t//\n434\t//\t// Step-by-step validation\n435\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n436\t//\t    \"Step 1: Check format - PASSED\",\n437\t//\t    \"Step 2: Check length - FAILED\",\n438\t//\t    \"Step 3: Check content - SKIPPED\",\n439\t//\t)\n440\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n441\t\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n442\t\treturn vf\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\t// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n446\t//\n447\t// This method allows you to provide domain-specific, actionable recommendations for\n448\t// resolving the validation error. Custom suggestions override the auto-generated\n449\t// suggestions that would normally be created based on the validation type and values.\n450\t//\n451\t// # Parameters\n452\t//\n453\t//   - suggestions: Variable number of suggestion strings\n454\t//\n455\t//     Best practices for suggestions:\n456\t//     - Be specific and actionable (not just \"check the value\")\n457\t//     - Provide concrete steps to fix the issue\n458\t//     - Include debugging tips when relevant\n459\t//     - Reference documentation or resources when applicable\n460\t//     - Keep suggestions concise but informative\n461\t//     - Order from most to least likely solution\n462\t//\n463\t// # When to Use Custom Suggestions\n464\t//\n465\t// Use this method when:\n466\t//   - Domain-specific knowledge is needed (not generic suggestions)\n467\t//   - You want to guide users to specific solutions\n468\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions would be too generic\n469\t//   - You need to reference internal documentation or systems\n470\t//   - The fix requires specific domain expertise\n471\t//\n472\t// # Returns\n473\t//\n474\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n475\t//\n476\t// # Example\n477\t//\n478\t//\t// Domain-specific suggestions\n479\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n480\t//\t    \"Check if the OAuth token has expired\",\n481\t//\t    \"Verify the token has 'read:users' scope\",\n482\t//\t    \"Contact admin to refresh permissions\",\n483\t//\t)\n484\t//\n485\t//\t// Specific debugging steps\n486\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n487\t//\t    \"Check service logs at /var/log/api/errors.log\",\n488\t//\t    \"Verify database connection in config.yaml\",\n489\t//\t    \"Test with curl: curl -v http://localhost:8080/health\",\n490\t//\t)\n491\t//\n492\t//\t// Reference documentation\n493\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n494\t//\t    \"See API docs: https://docs.example.com/errors#404\",\n495\t//\t    \"Check troubleshooting guide: /docs/troubleshooting.md\",\n496\t//\t)\n497\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n498\t\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n499\t\treturn vf\n500\t}\n501\t\n502\t// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n503\t//\n504\t// This is the final method in the builder chain that constructs the ValidationError\n505\t// with all the options that have been set. The returned ValidationError implements\n506\t// the error interface and can be returned directly or used in error handling.\n507\t//\n508\t// # Behavior\n509\t//\n510\t// The Format() method:\n511\t//   - Uses custom suggestions if provided via WithSuggestions\n512\t//   - Auto-generates suggestions if no custom ones were set\n513\t//   - Includes all fields that were set via With* methods\n514\t//   - Returns a ValidationError ready for use\n515\t//   - Implements error interface for immediate use\n516\t//\n517\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n518\t//\n519\t// If WithSuggestions was not called, suggestions are auto-generated based on:\n520\t//   - validationType (e.g., status_code gets endpoint-checking suggestions)\n521\t//   - expected and actual values (e.g., 404 gets resource-not-found suggestions)\n522\t//   - Common patterns for the validation category\n523\t//\n524\t// # Returns\n525\t//\n526\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all configured fields that implements\n527\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or\n528\t// test assertions.\n529\t//\n530\t// # Example\n531\t//\n532\t//\t// Complete usage example\n533\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n534\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n535\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n536\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n537\t//\t    Format()\n538\t//\n539\t//\t// Error() method will produce:\n540\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n541\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n542\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n543\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n544\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n545\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n546\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n547\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n548\t//\n549\t//\t// Can be used directly as an error\n550\t//\treturn err\n551\t//\n552\t//\t// Or formatted for logging\n553\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n554\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n555\t\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n556\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n557\t\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n558\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n559\t\t}\n560\t\n561\t\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n562\t\treturn ValidationError{\n563\t\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n564\t\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n565\t\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n566\t\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n567\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n568\t\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n569\t\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n570\t\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n571\t\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n572\t\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n573\t\t}\n574\t}\n575\t\n576\t// =============================================================================\n577\t// EXTENDING THE FORMATTER FOR NEW VALIDATION TYPES\n578\t// =============================================================================\n579\t//\n580\t// This section explains how to extend the ValidationFormatter to support new\n581\t// validation types beyond the built-in convenience functions.\n582\t//\n583\t// # Creating Custom Validation Types\n584\t//\n585\t// To add support for a new validation type, you have several options:\n586\t//\n587\t// ## Option 1: Use the Builder Pattern Directly\n588\t//\n589\t// The simplest approach is to use NewValidationFormatter with your custom type:\n590\t//\n591\t//\t// Custom header validation\n592\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n593\t//\t    WithExpected(\"X-Custom-Header: required\").\n594\t//\t    WithActual(\"missing\").\n595\t//\t    WithContext(\"API request validation\").\n596\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"X-Custom-Header\").\n597\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Add X-Custom-Header to requests\", \"See API docs\").\n598\t//\t    Format()\n599\t//\n600\t// ## Option 2: Create a Convenience Function\n601\t//\n602\t// For commonly used validation types, create a convenience function following\n603\t// the pattern of existing functions like FormatStatusCodeError:\n604\t//\n605\t//\t// FormatCustomHeaderError validates custom HTTP headers\n606\t//\tfunc FormatCustomHeaderError(headerName, expected, actual, context string) validate.ValidationError {\n607\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n608\t//\t        WithExpected(expected).\n609\t//\t        WithActual(actual).\n610\t//\t        WithFieldName(headerName).\n611\t//\t        WithContext(context).\n612\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n613\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Add %s header to request\", headerName),\n614\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected value: %s\", expected),\n615\t//\t        ).\n616\t//\t        Format()\n617\t//\t}\n618\t//\n619\t// ## Option 3: Use FormatCustomValidationError\n620\t//\n621\t// For advanced scenarios requiring full control, use FormatCustomValidationError:\n622\t//\n623\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n624\t//\t    \"response_time\",\n625\t//\t    \"< 100ms\",\n626\t//\t    \"250ms\",\n627\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"API performance check\"),\n628\t//\t    validate.WithValidationDetails(\"SLA requires < 100ms response time\"),\n629\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Optimize database queries\", \"Add caching\"),\n630\t//\t)\n631\t//\n632\t// # Best Practices for New Validation Types\n633\t//\n634\t// When creating custom validation types:\n635\t//\n636\t// 1. **Use descriptive type names**: Choose clear, descriptive validation type names\n637\t//    that indicate what was validated (e.g., \"response_time\", \"header_presence\").\n638\t//\n639\t// 2. **Provide specific suggestions**: Custom suggestions should be actionable and\n640\t//    specific to your domain rather than generic.\n641\t//\n642\t// 3. **Include relevant context**: Always provide context about where/when the\n643\t//    validation occurred to aid debugging.\n644\t//\n645\t// 4. **Use appropriate field types**: Match the expected/actual field types to what\n646\t//    you're validating (int for codes, string for patterns, etc.).\n647\t//\n648\t// 5. **Consider validation details**: Use WithValidationDetails for complex\n649\t//    validations with multiple steps or conditions.\n650\t//\n651\t// 6. **Document your validation type**: If creating a convenience function, document\n652\t//    it thoroughly like the existing convenience functions.\n653\t//\n654\t// # Integration with Auto-generated Suggestions\n655\t//\n656\t// The ValidationFormatter automatically generates suggestions based on validation\n657\t// type when you don't provide custom suggestions. To integrate your custom type\n658\t// with this system, you can modify the generateSuggestions function to recognize\n659\t// your new type and provide domain-appropriate suggestions.\n660\t//\n661\t// Current auto-generated suggestion patterns:\n662\t//   - \"status_code\": Endpoint and resource verification suggestions\n663\t//   - \"error_message\": Error analysis and debugging suggestions\n664\t//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n665\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n666\t//\n667\t// # Example: Adding a New Validation Type\n668\t//\n669\t// Here's a complete example of adding a \"response_time\" validation type:\n670\t//\n671\t//\t// 1. Create the convenience function\n672\t//\tfunc FormatResponseTimeError(expectedMaxMs, actualMs int, endpoint string) validate.ValidationError {\n673\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"response_time\").\n674\t//\t        WithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"< %dms\", expectedMaxMs)).\n675\t//\t        WithActual(fmt.Sprintf(\"%dms\", actualMs)).\n676\t//\t        WithContext(endpoint).\n677\t//\t        WithValidationDetails(\n678\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA requirement: < %dms\", expectedMaxMs),\n679\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual response time: %dms\", actualMs),\n680\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA violation: %dms over limit\", actualMs-expectedMaxMs),\n681\t//\t        ).\n682\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n683\t//\t            \"Check for slow database queries\",\n684\t//\t            \"Verify network latency\",\n685\t//\t            \"Review application performance metrics\",\n686\t//\t            \"Consider implementing caching\",\n687\t//\t        ).\n688\t//\t        Format()\n689\t//\t}\n690\t//\n691\t//\t// 2. Use it in your code\n692\t//\terr := FormatResponseTimeError(100, 250, \"GET /api/users\")\n693\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Performance validation failed: %v\", err)\n694\t//\n695\t// This approach ensures your custom validation types integrate seamlessly with\n696\t// the existing error formatting system while providing domain-specific guidance.\n697\t\n698\t// =============================================================================\n699\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n700\t// =============================================================================\n701\t\n702\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n703\t//\n704\t// Parameters:\n705\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n706\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n707\t//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n708\t//\n709\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n710\t//\n711\t// Example usage:\n712\t//\n713\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n714\t//\t// Output:\n715\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n716\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n717\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n718\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n719\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n720\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n721\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n722\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n723\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n724\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n725\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n726\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n727\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n728\t\t\tFormat()\n729\t}\n730\t\n731\t// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n732\t//\n733\t// Parameters:\n734\t//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n735\t//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n736\t//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n737\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n738\t//\n739\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n740\t//\n741\t// Example usage:\n742\t//\n743\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n744\t//\t// Output:\n745\t//\t// error_message validation failed\n746\t//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n747\t//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n748\t//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n749\t//\t//   Field:    error\n750\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n751\t//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n752\t//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n753\t//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\n754\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\n755\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n756\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n757\t\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n758\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n759\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n760\t\t\tFormat()\n761\t}\n762\t\n763\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n764\t//\n765\t// Parameters:\n766\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n767\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n768\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n769\t//\n770\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n771\t//\n772\t// Example usage:\n773\t//\n774\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\")\n775\t//\t// Output:\n776\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n777\t//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n778\t//\t//   Actual:   200\n779\t//\t//   Context:  error response check\n780\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n781\t//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n782\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n783\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n784\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n785\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n786\t\n787\t\t// Build range info string\n788\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n789\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n790\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n791\t\t}\n792\t\n793\t\tdetails := []string{}\n794\t\tif err != nil {\n795\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n796\t\t} else {\n797\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n798\t\t}\n799\t\n800\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n801\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n802\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n803\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n804\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n805\t\n806\t\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n807\t\tif err == nil {\n808\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n809\t\t}\n810\t\n811\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n812\t}\n813\t\n814\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n815\t//\n816\t// Parameters:\n817\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n818\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n819\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n820\t//\n821\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n822\t//\n823\t// Example usage:\n824\t//\n825\t//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n826\t//\t// Output:\n827\t//\t// content_type validation failed\n828\t//\t//   Expected: application/json\n829\t//\t//   Actual:   text/html\n830\t//   Context:  API response\n831\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n832\t//\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n833\t//\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n834\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n835\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\n836\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n837\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n838\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n839\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n840\t\t\tFormat()\n841\t}\n842\t\n843\t// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n844\t//\n845\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n846\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n847\t// your specific requirements.\n848\t//\n849\t// Parameters:\n850\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n851\t//   - expected: The expected value\n852\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n853\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n854\t//\n855\t// Example usage:\n856\t//\n857\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n858\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n859\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n860\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n861\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n862\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n863\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n864\t//\t)\n865\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n866\t\tvalidationType string,\n867\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n868\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n869\t) ValidationError {\n870\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n871\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n872\t\t\topt(config)\n873\t\t}\n874\t\n875\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n876\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n877\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n878\t\n879\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n880\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n881\t\t}\n882\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n883\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n884\t\t}\n885\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n886\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n887\t\t}\n888\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n889\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n890\t\t}\n891\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n892\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n893\t\t}\n894\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n895\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n896\t\t}\n897\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n898\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n899\t\t}\n900\t\n901\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n902\t}\n903\t\n904\t// =============================================================================\n905\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n906\t// =============================================================================\n907\t\n908\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n909\ttype FormatConfig struct {\n910\t\tContext            string\n911\t\tResponseSnippet    string\n912\t\tFieldName          string\n913\t\tPatternDetails     string\n914\t\tRangeInfo          string\n915\t\tValidationDetails  []string\n916\t\tSuggestions        []string\n917\t\t// Category-aware and severity support\n918\t\tSeverityOverride   ErrorSeverity\n919\t\tCategoryHint       ErrorCategory\n920\t\tStatusCode         int\n921\t\tTimeout            int\n922\t\tSecurityContext    string\n923\t}\n924\t\n925\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n926\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n927\t\n928\t// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\n929\tfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\n930\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n931\t\t\tc.Context = context\n932\t\t}\n933\t}\n934\t\n935\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n936\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n937\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n938\t\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n939\t\t}\n940\t}\n941\t\n942\t// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\n943\tfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n944\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n945\t\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n946\t\t}\n947\t}\n948\t\n949\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\n950\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n951\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n952\t\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n953\t\t}\n954\t}\n955\t\n956\t// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\n957\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n958\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n959\t\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n960\t\t}\n961\t}\n962\t\n963\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\n964\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n965\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n966\t\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n967\t\t}\n968\t}\n969\t\n970\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\n971\tfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n972\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n973\t\t\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\n974\t\t}\n975\t}\n976\t\n977\t// WithSeverityOverride creates a FormatOption that overrides the default severity level.\n978\t// Use this when you want to specify a custom severity instead of using the error type's default.\n979\t//\n980\t// Example usage:\n981\t//\n982\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n983\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n984\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n985\t//\t    \"email\",\n986\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n987\t//\t)\n988\tfunc WithSeverityOverride(severity ErrorSeverity) FormatOption {\n989\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n990\t\t\tc.SeverityOverride = severity\n991\t\t}\n992\t}\n993\t\n994\t// WithCategoryHint creates a FormatOption that provides a category hint for formatting.\n995\t// Use this when you want to specify a custom category instead of using the error type's default.\n996\t//\n997\t// Example usage:\n998\t//\n999\t//\terr := validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n1004\t//\t)\n1005\tfunc WithCategoryHint(category ErrorCategory) FormatOption {\n1006\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1007\t\t\tc.CategoryHint = category\n1008\t\t}\n1009\t}\n1010\t\n1011\t// WithStatusCode creates a FormatOption that sets the HTTP status code for HTTP errors.\n1012\t// Use this with HTTP-related errors to include status code information in the formatted message.\n1013\t//\n1014\t// Example usage:\n1015\t//\n1016\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1017\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1018\t//\t    \"Resource not found\",\n1019\t//\t    \"endpoint\",\n1020\t//\t    validate.WithStatusCode(404),\n1021\t//\t)\n1022\tfunc WithStatusCode(statusCode int) FormatOption {\n1023\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1024\t\t\tc.StatusCode = statusCode\n1025\t\t}\n1026\t}\n1027\t\n1028\t// WithTimeout creates a FormatOption that sets the timeout for performance errors.\n1029\t// Use this with performance-related errors to include timeout information in the formatted message.\n1030\t//\n1031\t// Example usage:\n1032\t//\n1033\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1034\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1035\t//\t    \"Request timed out\",\n1036\t//\t    \"api_response\",\n1037\t//\t    validate.WithTimeout(5000),\n1038\t//\t)\n1039\tfunc WithTimeout(timeout int) FormatOption {\n1040\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1041\t\t\tc.Timeout = timeout\n1042\t\t}\n1043\t}\n1044\t\n1045\t// WithSecurityContext creates a FormatOption that sets the security context for security errors.\n1046\t// Use this with security-related errors to include security context in the formatted message.\n1047\t//\n1048\t// Example usage:\n1049\t//\n1050\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1051\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1052\t//\t    \"Invalid credentials\",\n1053\t//\t    \"Authorization\",\n1054\t//\t    validate.WithSecurityContext(\"authentication\"),\n1055\t//\t)\n1056\tfunc WithSecurityContext(securityContext string) FormatOption {\n1057\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1058\t\t\tc.SecurityContext = securityContext\n1059\t\t}\n1060\t}\n1061\t\n1062\t\n1063\t// =============================================================================\n1064\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n1065\t// =============================================================================\n1066\t\n1067\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n1068\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n1069\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n1070\t\tif err != nil {\n1071\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n1072\t\t}\n1073\t\n1074\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n1075\t\tif err != nil {\n1076\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n1077\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n1078\t\t}\n1079\t\n1080\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n1081\t}\n1082\t\n1083\t// =============================================================================\n1084\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n1085\t// =============================================================================\n1086\t\n1087\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n1088\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n1089\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n1090\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n1091\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n1092\t}\n1093\t\n1094\t// global tracker instance\n1095\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n1096\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n1097\t}\n1098\t\n1099\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n1100\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n1101\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n1102\t//\n1103\t// Parameters:\n1104\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n1105\t//\n1106\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n1107\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n1108\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1109\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1110\t\n1111\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n1112\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n1113\t}\n1114\t\n1115\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n1116\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n1117\t// configuration issues.\n1118\t//\n1119\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n1120\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n1121\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1122\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1123\t\n1124\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n1125\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n1126\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n1127\t\t}\n1128\t\treturn result\n1129\t}\n1130\t\n1131\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n1132\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n1133\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n1134\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1135\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1136\t\n1137\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n1138\t}\n1139\t\n1140\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n1141\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n1142\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n1143\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1144\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1145\t\n1146\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n1147\t}\n1148\t\n1149\t// =============================================================================\n1150\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n1151\t// =============================================================================\n1152\t\n1153\t// FormatError creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum.\n1154\t// This is the primary error formatting function that provides type-safe error classification.\n1155\t//\n1156\t// This function uses ErrorType enum for consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n1157\t// For backward compatibility with string-based error types, use FormatErrorString.\n1158\t//\n1159\t// ErrorType to Message Mapping:\n1160\t// The ErrorType enum provides type-safe error classification with the following mappings:\n1161\t//   - ErrTypeRequired (required): Required field is missing or empty\n1162\t//   - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n1163\t//   - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n1164\t//   - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n1165\t//   - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n1166\t//   - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n1167\t//   - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n1168\t//   - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n1169\t//   - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n1170\t//\n1171\t// Parameters:\n1172\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1173\t//   - message: The error message content\n1174\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1175\t//\n1176\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure: \"[errorType] fieldName: message\"\n1177\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1178\t//\n1179\t// Example usage:\n1180\t//\n1181\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1182\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1183\t//\n1184\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1185\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1186\t//\n1187\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n1188\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n1189\t//\n1190\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\", \"email\")\n1191\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: email validation failed\" (empty message fallback)\n1192\tfunc FormatError(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1193\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n1194\t\t// This converts the typed ErrorType enum to its string representation\n1195\t\t// for consistent message formatting across the codebase\n1196\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1197\t\n1198\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n1199\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1200\t\n1201\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1202\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1203\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n1204\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1205\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1206\t\t\t} else {\n1207\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1208\t\t\t}\n1209\t\t}\n1210\t\n1211\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n1212\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n1213\t}\n1214\t\n1215\t// FormatErrorString creates a formatted error message using string error type.\n1216\t// This function provides backward compatibility for code that uses string-based error types.\n1217\t//\n1218\t// This function validates string error types against the ErrorType enum for type-safe\n1219\t// error classification while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value.\n1220\t//\n1221\t// Error Type Validation:\n1222\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n1223\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n1224\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n1225\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1226\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1227\t//\n1228\t// Parameters:\n1229\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error as a string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n1230\t//   - message: The error message content\n1231\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (variadic parameter)\n1232\t//\n1233\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n1234\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1235\t//\n1236\t// Example usage:\n1237\t//\n1238\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n1239\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1240\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1241\t//\n1242\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\")\n1243\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1244\t//\n1245\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n1246\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n1247\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n1248\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n1249\t//\n1250\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n1251\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1252\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n1253\tfunc FormatErrorString(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n1254\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n1255\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n1256\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n1257\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n1258\t\t}\n1259\t\n1260\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n1261\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n1262\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n1263\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n1264\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n1265\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n1266\t\n1267\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n1268\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n1269\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1270\t\t}\n1271\t\n1272\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n1273\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n1274\t\n1275\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n1276\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n1277\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n1278\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n1279\t\n1280\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n1281\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n1282\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n1283\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n1284\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1285\t\t}\n1286\t\n1287\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n1288\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1289\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n1290\t\n1291\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1292\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1293\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n1294\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n1295\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n1296\t\t\t} else {\n1297\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1298\t\t\t}\n1299\t\t}\n1300\t\n1301\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n1302\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n1303\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n1304\t\t}\n1305\t\n1306\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n1307\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n1308\t}\n1309\t\n1310\t// FormatErrorWithType is an alias for FormatError for backward compatibility.\n1311\t// This function calls FormatError with the same parameters.\n1312\t//\n1313\t// Deprecated: Use FormatError directly with ErrorType enum parameter instead.\n1314\t// This function is maintained for backward compatibility with existing code.\n1315\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1316\t\treturn FormatError(errorType, message, fieldName)\n1317\t}\n1318\t\n1319\t// =============================================================================\n1320\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n1321\t// =============================================================================\n1322\t\n1323\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n1324\ttype QuoteStyle string\n1325\t\n1326\tconst (\n1327\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n1328\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n1329\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n1330\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n1331\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n1332\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n1333\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n1334\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n1335\t)\n1336\t\n1337\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n1338\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n1339\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n1340\t\tprefix     string\n1341\t}\n1342\t\n1343\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n1344\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n1345\t\n1346\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n1347\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n1348\t//\n1349\t// Example usage:\n1350\t//\n1351\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1352\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1353\t//\n1354\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1355\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1356\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n1357\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1358\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n1359\t\t}\n1360\t}\n1361\t\n1362\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n1363\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n1364\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n1365\t//\n1366\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n1367\t//\n1368\t// Example usage:\n1369\t//\n1370\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n1371\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n1372\t//\n1373\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n1374\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n1375\t//\n1376\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1377\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n1378\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n1379\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1380\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n1381\t\t}\n1382\t}\n1383\t\n1384\t// =============================================================================\n1385\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n1386\t// =============================================================================\n1387\t\n1388\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n1389\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n1390\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n1391\t//\n1392\t// Parameters:\n1393\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1394\t//\n1395\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n1396\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n1397\t//\n1398\t// Example usage:\n1399\t//\n1400\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n1401\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n1402\t//\n1403\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n1404\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1405\t//\n1406\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n1407\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n1408\t//\n1409\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n1410\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1411\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n1412\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1413\t\n1414\t\t// Handle empty path\n1415\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1416\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1417\t\t}\n1418\t\n1419\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n1420\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1421\t}\n1422\t\n1423\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n1424\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n1425\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n1426\t\tif path == \"\" {\n1427\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1428\t\t}\n1429\t\n1430\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n1431\t\tvar result []string\n1432\t\n1433\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n1434\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n1435\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n1436\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1437\t\t\t}\n1438\t\n1439\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n1440\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n1441\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n1442\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n1443\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n1444\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n1445\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n1446\t\t\t\t} else {\n1447\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n1448\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1449\t\t\t\t}\n1450\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n1451\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n1452\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1453\t\t\t} else {\n1454\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n1455\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1456\t\t\t}\n1457\t\t}\n1458\t\n1459\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n1460\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1461\t\t}\n1462\t\n1463\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n1464\t}\n1465\t\n1466\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n1467\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n1468\t\tif s == \"\" {\n1469\t\t\treturn false\n1470\t\t}\n1471\t\n1472\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n1473\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n1474\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n1475\t\t\t\treturn false\n1476\t\t\t}\n1477\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n1478\t\t}\n1479\t\n1480\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n1481\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n1482\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n1483\t\t\t\treturn false\n1484\t\t\t}\n1485\t\t}\n1486\t\n1487\t\treturn true\n1488\t}\n1489\t\n1490\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n1491\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n1492\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n1493\t}\n1494\t\n1495\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n1496\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n1497\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n1498\t// for field names through variadic options.\n1499\t//\n1500\t// Parameters:\n1501\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1502\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n1503\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1504\t//\n1505\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n1506\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n1507\t//\n1508\t// Example usage:\n1509\t//\n1510\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n1511\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n1512\t//\n1513\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n1514\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1515\t//\n1516\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n1517\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n1518\t//\n1519\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n1520\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n1521\t//\n1522\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1523\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1524\t//\n1525\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1526\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1527\t//\n1528\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n1529\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n1530\t//\n1531\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n1532\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1533\t//\n1534\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n1535\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n1536\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n1537\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1538\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n1539\t\n1540\t\t// Apply options\n1541\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n1542\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1543\t\t\topt(config)\n1544\t\t}\n1545\t\n1546\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n1547\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n1548\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n1549\t\t}\n1550\t\n1551\t\t// Handle empty field path\n1552\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1553\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1554\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n1555\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1556\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n1557\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n1558\t\t\t\t}\n1559\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1560\t\t\t}\n1561\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1562\t\t}\n1563\t\n1564\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n1565\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1566\t\n1567\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n1568\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1569\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1570\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1571\t\t\t}\n1572\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1573\t\t}\n1574\t\n1575\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n1576\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n1577\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n1578\t\t}\n1579\t\n1580\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n1581\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1582\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n1583\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1584\t\n1585\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n1586\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1587\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1588\t\t\t}\n1589\t\n1590\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n1591\t\t}\n1592\t\n1593\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1594\t}\n1595\t\n1596\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n1597\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n1598\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n1599\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n1600\t\t\treturn path\n1601\t\t}\n1602\t\n1603\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n1604\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n1605\t\tinBrackets := false\n1606\t\n1607\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n1608\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n1609\t\n1610\t\t\tswitch {\n1611\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n1612\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n1613\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1614\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1615\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1616\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1617\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1618\t\t\t\t}\n1619\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n1620\t\n1621\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n1622\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n1623\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1624\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1625\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1626\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1627\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1628\t\t\t\t}\n1629\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n1630\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n1631\t\n1632\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n1633\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n1634\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n1635\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n1636\t\n1637\t\t\tdefault:\n1638\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n1639\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n1640\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n1641\t\t\t\t} else {\n1642\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n1643\t\t\t\t}\n1644\t\t\t}\n1645\t\t}\n1646\t\n1647\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n1648\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1649\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1650\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1651\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1652\t\t}\n1653\t\n1654\t\treturn result.String()\n1655\t}\n1656\t\n1657\t// =============================================================================\n1658\t// CATEGORY-AWARE FORMATTING WITH SEVERITY\n1659\t// =============================================================================\n1660\t\n1661\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum\n1662\t// with explicit severity support. This function provides type-safe error classification\n1663\t// with severity-based formatting.\n1664\t//\n1665\t// This function respects error severity levels and applies appropriate formatting:\n1666\t// - Critical errors: Bold styling with prominent indicators\n1667\t// - High errors: Warning indicators with emphasis\n1668\t// - Medium errors: Standard formatting with indicators\n1669\t// - Low errors: Subtle formatting\n1670\t// - Info errors: Minimal formatting\n1671\t//\n1672\t// Parameters:\n1673\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1674\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level (e.g., SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh)\n1675\t//   - message: The error message content\n1676\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1677\t//\n1678\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity-based styling.\n1679\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1680\t//\n1681\t// Example usage:\n1682\t//\n1683\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeRequired, validate.SeverityHigh, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1684\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] HIGH [required] email: This field is required\"\n1685\t//\n1686\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeFormat, validate.SeverityMedium, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1687\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] MED [format] Invalid email format\"\n1688\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1689\t\t// Get default severity if not provided\n1690\t\tif severity == \"\" {\n1691\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1692\t\t}\n1693\t\n1694\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1695\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1696\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1697\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1698\t\n1699\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1700\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1701\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1702\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1703\t\t\t} else {\n1704\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1705\t\t\t}\n1706\t\t}\n1707\t\n1708\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1709\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1710\t\n1711\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1712\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1713\t\n1714\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1715\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1716\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1717\t\n1718\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1719\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorTypeStr))\n1720\t\n1721\t\t// Add field name if present\n1722\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1723\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1724\t\t}\n1725\t\n1726\t\t// Add message\n1727\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1728\t\n1729\t\treturn builder.String()\n1730\t}\n1731\t\n1732\t// formatSeverityPrefix creates a severity prefix with indicator and tag.\n1733\tfunc formatSeverityPrefix(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1734\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n1735\t\ttag := severityTag(severity)\n1736\t\n1737\t\tif indicator != \"\" && tag != \"\" {\n1738\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s %s]\", indicator, tag)\n1739\t\t} else if indicator != \"\" {\n1740\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", indicator)\n1741\t\t} else if tag != \"\" {\n1742\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", tag)\n1743\t\t}\n1744\t\n1745\t\treturn \"\"\n1746\t}\n1747\t\n1748\t// severityTag returns a short tag prefix for severity levels.\n1749\tfunc severityTag(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1750\t\tswitch severity {\n1751\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n1752\t\t\treturn \"CRIT\"\n1753\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n1754\t\t\treturn \"HIGH\"\n1755\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n1756\t\t\treturn \"MED\"\n1757\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n1758\t\t\treturn \"LOW\"\n1759\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n1760\t\t\treturn \"INFO\"\n1761\t\tdefault:\n1762\t\t\treturn \"UNK\"\n1763\t\t}\n1764\t}\n1765\t\n1766\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a formatted error message with\n1767\t// both category and severity awareness. This is the most comprehensive formatting\n1768\t// function that respects both error categorization and severity levels.\n1769\t//\n1770\t// This function applies category-specific formatting:\n1771\t// - HTTP errors: Technical focus with status code details\n1772\t// - Content errors: Response structure and body focus\n1773\t// - Validation errors: Field-level validation focus\n1774\t// - Performance errors: Timing and rate limit focus\n1775\t// - Security errors: Authentication and authorization focus\n1776\t//\n1777\t// Combined with severity-based styling for maximum clarity.\n1778\t//\n1779\t// Parameters:\n1780\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n1781\t//   - message: The error message content\n1782\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n1783\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization\n1784\t//\n1785\t// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity awareness.\n1786\t//\n1787\t// Example usage:\n1788\t//\n1789\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1790\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1791\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n1792\t//\t    \"email\",\n1793\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n1794\t//\t    validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n1795\t//\t)\n1796\t//\t// Returns: \"[🚨 CRIT] [Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n1797\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1798\t\terrorType ErrorType,\n1799\t\tmessage string,\n1800\t\tfieldName string,\n1801\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n1802\t) string {\n1803\t\t// Get default category and severity\n1804\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1805\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1806\t\n1807\t\t// Apply options to override defaults\n1808\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n1809\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1810\t\t\topt(config)\n1811\t\t}\n1812\t\n1813\t\t// Apply overrides if specified\n1814\t\tif config.SeverityOverride != \"\" {\n1815\t\t\tseverity = config.SeverityOverride\n1816\t\t}\n1817\t\tif config.CategoryHint != \"\" {\n1818\t\t\tcategory = config.CategoryHint\n1819\t\t}\n1820\t\n1821\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1822\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1823\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1824\t\n1825\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1826\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1827\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1828\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1829\t\t\t} else {\n1830\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1831\t\t\t}\n1832\t\t}\n1833\t\n1834\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1835\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1836\t\n1837\t\t// Build category-specific formatting\n1838\t\tcategoryPrefix := formatCategoryPrefix(category)\n1839\t\n1840\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1841\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1842\t\n1843\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1844\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1845\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1846\t\n1847\t\t// Add category prefix if category is not custom\n1848\t\tif category != CategoryCustom && category != \"\" {\n1849\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(categoryPrefix)\n1850\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1851\t\t}\n1852\t\n1853\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1854\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n1855\t\n1856\t\t// Add field name if present\n1857\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1858\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1859\t\t}\n1860\t\n1861\t\t// Add message\n1862\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1863\t\n1864\t\t// Add category-specific suffix if applicable\n1865\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatCategorySuffix(category, config))\n1866\t\n1867\t\treturn builder.String()\n1868\t}\n1869\t\n1870\t// formatCategoryPrefix creates a category-specific prefix.\n1871\tfunc formatCategoryPrefix(category ErrorCategory) string {\n1872\t\tswitch category {\n1873\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1874\t\t\treturn \"[HTTP]\"\n1875\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n1876\t\t\treturn \"[Content]\"\n1877\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n1878\t\t\treturn \"[Validation]\"\n1879\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1880\t\t\treturn \"[Performance]\"\n1881\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n1882\t\t\treturn \"[Security]\"\n1883\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n1884\t\t\treturn \"[Custom]\"\n1885\t\tdefault:\n1886\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1887\t\t}\n1888\t}\n1889\t\n1890\t// formatCategorySuffix creates a category-specific suffix with additional context.\n1891\tfunc formatCategorySuffix(category ErrorCategory, config *FormatConfig) string {\n1892\t\t// Handle nil config gracefully\n1893\t\tif config == nil {\n1894\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1895\t\t}\n1896\t\n1897\t\tvar suffix strings.Builder\n1898\t\n1899\t\tswitch category {\n1900\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n1901\t\t\t// Add HTTP-specific context\n1902\t\t\tif config.StatusCode != 0 {\n1903\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (status: %d)\", config.StatusCode))\n1904\t\t\t}\n1905\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n1906\t\t\t// Add performance-specific context\n1907\t\t\tif config.Timeout > 0 {\n1908\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" fieldName))\n1989\t\t}\n1990\t\n1991\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1992\t\n1993\t\tif expected != nil || actual != nil {\n1994\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n1995\t\t\tif expected != nil \")\n2000\t\t\t}\n2001\t\t\tif actual != nil {\n2002\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\",
